citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Appendix B: Suggested Language for Notice of Missed Interview
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Appendix B: Suggested Language for Notice of Missed Interview

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Appendix B: Suggested Language for Notice of Missed Interview

body:
You did not keep your interview appointment for SNAP benefits on ________.

It is your obligation to call and ask for another interview no later than ________.

If you fail to keep an interview appointment, your application will be denied.

7 CFR § 273.2(1)(3)

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing March 12, 2008

heading: Appendix B: Suggested Language for Notice of Missed Interview

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 505: Authorized Representative
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 505: Authorized Representative

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 505: Authorized Representative

body:
Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Handbook

Chapter 505: Authorized Representative
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 505: Authorized Representative 1
heading: 505 Title

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 505: Authorized Representative

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.1 General Policy
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.1 General Policy

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.1 General Policy

body:
Any responsible member of the household may choose an AR to apply for, get, or use SNAP benefits for the household.

The designated AR who knows the household's situation may complete the SNAP interview for the household.

7 CFR § 273.2(n)

The CAO must encourage the household to name an AR when applying.

The CAO must write the name of the AR in the household's case file on the Household screen as the Second Payment name in eCIS . There are no limits on the number of households an AR may represent. The CAO must make sure that:

Each household has freely asked for the assistance of the AR;

The request for an AR is in writing;

The household’s circumstances are correctly represented;

7 CFR § 273.2(n)(1)

The household is getting the correct amount of benefits; and

7 CFR § 273.2(n)(4)

The AR is properly using the benefits.

Updated January 21, 2025, replacing May 8, 2024
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.1 General Policy 1
heading: 505.1 General Policy

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.1 General Policy

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.2 Authorized Representative Applying for a Household
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.2 Authorized Representative Applying for a Household

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.2 Authorized Representative Applying for a Household

body:
The AR chosen to make an application for the household must be:

7 CFR § 273.2(n)(1)(i)

An adult who is not a member of the household;

Designated in writing by the head of household, the spouse, or another responsible member of the household, and

Aware of the household’s circumstances.

The designated AR may complete the SNAP interview for the household.

The household is responsible for any overissuance resulting from wrong information the AR gives (see Chapter 504 ).

The household is not responsible for wrong information given by an AR chosen by a drug or alcohol treatment center or a group living arrangement.

7 CFR § 273.2(n)(1)

An AR may complete work-registration forms for household members who are required to register.

Updated January 21, 2025, replacing May 8 , 2024
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.2 Authorized Representative Applying for a Household 1
heading: 505.2 Authorized Representative Applying for a Household

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.2 Authorized Representative Applying for a Household

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.3 Authorized Representative Obtaining Benefits
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.3 Authorized Representative Obtaining Benefits

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.3 Authorized Representative Obtaining Benefits

body:
The CAO must encourage households to name an AR in case of illness or other reasons that keep them from using the EBT system.

7 CFR § 273.2(n)(2)

The AR chosen by the household to access benefits in the EBT system may be the same AR used to apply for benefits (or to meet reporting requirements) or may be another person designated anytime during the certification period .

The household will use the Authorized Representative Request form in Appendix A to designate an AR. T he CAO must have the following required information about the AR:

First name, middle initial, and last name

Social Security number (Optional)

Birth date

Street address

The CAO must issue a Pennsylvania EBT ACCESS card to the AR for each household the AR represents. The AR must choose a personal identification number (PIN) , either at the CAO or by telephone.

7 CFR § 274.2(f)

NOTE: The CAO must create the primary card in the case payment name before creating a card for the AR.

If the head of household is not present to choose a PIN for the primary card, the AR must deliver this "unpinned" card to the household so that household members can use the card for Medical Assistance.

The head of household may select a PIN for the primary card by telephone or at the CAO at a later date.

The EBT card for an AR remains active until the household cancels it or a new card replaces it. The head of household may call the EBT Recipient Hotline at 1-888-328-7366 any time to cancel or disable the AR’s EBT card. The cancellation takes place immediately. If the household chooses a new AR, the old EBT card is automatically cancelled when the CAO requests a replacement EBT card for the new AR.

Updated March 4, 2025, replacing May 8, 2024
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.3 Authorized Representative Obtaining Benefits 1
heading: 505.3 Authorized Representative Getting Benefits

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.3 Authorized Representative Obtaining Benefits

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.4 Authorized Representative Using EBT
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.4 Authorized Representative Using EBT

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.4 Authorized Representative Using EBT

body:
The AR may use the PA EBT ACCESS card at any authorized retail food outlet to buy food for the household .

7 CFR 274.8(b)(10)

The EBT system tracks every transaction by the primary account number, creating an electronic record of every purchase the AR makes.

7 CFR 274.8(a)(1)(iv)

Since the recipient may also access the EBT account, the electronic record may help to resolve disputes between the recipient and the AR.

The CAO must provide a copy of the transaction log if the recipient asks for one. The EBT system keeps an online record of transactions for the most recent 90 days. A special request to the eCIS hotline is required for older records. The logs are usually available in about two weeks.

7 CFR 274.2(g)(2)(i)

Updated May 8, 2024, replacing July
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.4 Authorized Representative Using EBT 1
heading: 505.4 Authorized Representative Using EBT

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.4 Authorized Representative Using EBT

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.5 Authorized Representative for a Resident of a Drug or Alcohol Treatment Center
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.5 Authorized Representative for a Resident of a Drug or Alcohol Treatment Center

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.5 Authorized Representative for a Resident of a Drug or Alcohol Treatment Center

body:
The center must designate an employee to apply for and get SNAP benefits for each resident, including any children living with the resident.

7 CFR 273.11(e)(1)

NOTE: The AR must get a separate PA EBT ACCESS card for each recipient household he or she represents.

The center may designate a staff person to be the AR for residents of that facility. In that case, the CAO must use the facility’s tax identification number rather than the AR's Social Security number for the PA EBT ACCESS card.

The center may also choose to issue the AR's EBT ACCESS card in the agency’s name. This allows for staff turnover and for assigning more than one person to assist the recipient during the month. To safeguard benefit security and accountability, the community agency should be a nonprofit agency that gives personal one-on-one help to clients. Area Agencies on Aging, mental health/mental retardation agencies, and homemaker agencies are examples.

Agencies requesting an AR designation must have the following:

A written confidentiality policy that employees and volunteers agree to follow

A bond to cover the persons who provide the service

Management supervision

Oversight of the service

The center will access benefits in the EBT account for food prepared by or served to its residents and comply with all requirements in Chapter 511 .

7 CFR 273.11(f)(8)

Updated May 8, 2024, replacing March 1, 2012
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.5 Authorized Representative for a Resident of a Drug or Alcohol Treatment Center 1
heading: 505.5 Authorized Representative for a Resident of a Drug or Alcohol Treatment Center

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.5 Authorized Representative for a Resident of a Drug or Alcohol Treatment Center

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.6 Authorized Representative for a Resident of a Group Living Arrangement
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.6 Authorized Representative for a Resident of a Group Living Arrangement

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.6 Authorized Representative for a Resident of a Group Living Arrangement

body:
A resident of a group living arrangement (GLA) may apply:

As a one-person household ;

Through an AR of his or her choice; or

As a one-person household using a GLA-designated employee as the AR.

The GLA must decide if a resident may apply on his or her own. The GLA must first consult with any other state agencies that provide services to the resident and then decide whether the resident is physically and mentally able to handle his or her own affairs.

Not all residents of the GLA have to be certified through an AR or individually for one or the other methods to be used. The GLA may choose a staff member to be the AR or have the GLA act as an AR. See Section 505.5 .

7 CFR § 273.11(f)(1)

If the GLA is the AR, it may:

Access the benefits for food prepared by or served to the eligible resident;

Allow the eligible resident to access the benefits himself; or

Buy prepared meals from authorized restaurants serving the homeless, elderly, or disabled.

If a resident is certified individually, the PA EBT ACCESS card may be used:

7 CFR § 273.11(f)

To buy food for meals served communally or individually to the resident;

By the resident to buy and prepare food for his or her own consumption;

To buy meals prepared and served by the GLA; or

To buy meals from authorized restaurants serving the homeless, elderly, or disabled.

7 CFR 273.11(f)(8)

NOTE: For more information on GLAs, see Chapter 511 .

Updated May 8, 2024, replacing March 1, 2012

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.7 Authorized Representative for Multiple Households
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.7 Authorized Representative for Multiple Households

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.7 Authorized Representative for Multiple Households

body:
An AR may represent any number of households. When an employer acts as an AR for migrant or seasonal farm workers, or when a single representative has access to benefits from multiple households, the CAO must make sure that:

The household has freely asked for an AR;

The household’s circumstances are correctly represented;

The household is getting the correct amount of benefits; and

The AR is using the benefits properly.

7 CFR § 273.2(n)(4)(ii)(iii)

Reviewed May 8, 2024, replacing March 1, 2012

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.8 Restrictions
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.8 Restrictions

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 505.8 Restrictions

body:
A person who is disqualified from participating in SNAP because of an intentional program violation may act as an AR during the disqualification period only if there is no other adult in the household and the CAO cannot arrange for another AR.

7 CFR 273.2(n)(4)(i)(B)

A person classified as a nonhousehold member may be an AR for the household only if the person is an adult who knows enough about the relevant household circumstances and the AR request was made in writing by the head of household, the spouse of the head of household, or another responsible member of the household.

7 CFR § 273.2(n)(1)(i)

A retailer authorized to accept SNAP benefits or a CAO employee involved in certifying or issuing benefits may act as an AR only if the executive director (or his or her designee ) gives written approval and determines that no one else is available to serve as an AR.

NOTE: A provider of meals for the homeless may not act as an AR for a homeless SNAP recipient.

7 CFR § 273.2(n)(4)(i)(D)

Updated May 8, 2024, replacing March 1, 2012

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 506: Expedited Service
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 506: Expedited Service

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 506: Expedited Service

body:
Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Handbook

Chapter 506: Expedited Service
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 506: Expedited Service 1
heading: 506 Title

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 506: Expedited Service

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.1 General Policy
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.1 General Policy

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.1 General Policy

body:
PFS-22218-506 , Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Expedited Processing Procedure for Pending Information from Previous Expedited SNAP Issuance (Published July 18, 2025)

According to Federal regulations, there are two standards for issuing Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) benefits: normal processing and expedited processing. The requirement to provide initial benefits within 30 days applies to households subject to normal processing standards. Households who meet expedited service criteria must be able to get their initial benefits within 5 calendar days.

Expedited service is an important SNAP policy for households who need benefits right away . An expedited service household may not choose to have its application processed in the normal time frame. An eligible applicant household that meets expedited service criteria must receive (and have a reasonable opportunity to use) its first benefit no later than the 5th calendar day after the application filing date .

7 CFR § 273.2(i)

A household requesting, but not entitled to, expedited service must have its application processed in the normal time frame.

7 CFR §273.2(i)(4)(v)

506.11 How Expedited Service Works

The County Assistance Office ( CAO) must conduct a preliminary review of every identifiable application on the day it receives the application to see if the household meets one of the criteria for expedited service.

7 CFR § 273.2(i)(2)

NOTE: An identifiable application is one that contains the applicant’s name, address, and signature.

Every application is subject to a preliminary review, the first result of which should be to determine whether the application is from an applicant or recipient household.

An applicant household- is a one-person or multi-person household which has applied for benefits the household currently does not receive. An applicant household must be screened for expedited SNAP.

A recipient household- is a household currently receiving SNAP and is applying for a different benefit or adding on a new member to the SNAP benefit. A recipient household does not need to be screened for expedited SNAP.

The CAO must include the preliminary review of recipient households in the narrative and include that an Expedited SNAP review was not applicable.

NOTE: There are some instances when an application may be received from an individual who is not receiving SNAP benefits but lives in a household with individuals who are receiving benefits. In these circumstances, the individual who is not receiving benefits may be screened for expedited service eligibility. The individuals who have already received benefits for the month in another household would be added to the budget the first of the following month.

NOTE : An application for benefits may be any form approved by the Department of Human Services ( DHS) for SNAP : the Pennsylvania Application for Benefits ( PA 600 ) or the Pennsylvania Application for the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) ( PA 600 FS ) or a Commonwealth of Pennsylvania Access to Social Services ( COMPASS) online application. If the household submits an application not approved for SNAP or a COMPASS application requesting only Medical Assistance, a second application is required. The effective date for expedited SNAP benefits is the date of the second application.

The CAO must screen every household for expedited service including households that have voluntarily withdrawn their application . A household may not waive this screening

The CAO may combine the preliminary review and the application pre screening only if both can be completed on the day the CAO gets the application for benefits.

If the CAO terminates SNAP benefits during a certification period and the household asks to be reinstated, a review for expedited services is not required. The CAO may reinstate benefits until the end of the original recertification period.

7 CFR §273.2(i)(4)(iv)

The CAO must narrate how it determines eligibility/ineligibility and benefit levels. The narrative should be sufficiently detailed so that a reviewer can confirm the reasonableness and accuracy of the determination.

Updated March 12, 2026, replacing April 11, 2024
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.1 General Policy 1
heading: 506.1 General Policy

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.1 General Policy

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.2 Application Filing
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.2 Application Filing

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.2 Application Filing

body:
506.2 CAO Responsibilities at Application

The County Assitance Office ( CAO) must treat every application as if the household is entitled to expedited service .

506.21 CAO Responsibilities

The CAO must do the following:

Make sure receptionists, caseworkers, and supervisors are aware of policy and procedures for expedited services.

Make sure CAO staff that have contact with potential Supplemental Nutrition Assitance Program (SNAP) households are trained to give correct information on how to apply.

Give all applicants the right to apply or be screened for expedited service.

Encourage people who contact the CAO about SNAP benefits to apply on that day.

Accept and date-stamp upon receipt any application for benefits that has the name, address, and signature of the applicant.

NOTE: SNAP applications received with or without an e-signature or initials by the community partners listed in SNAP Handbook 503 Appendix C are considered an identifiable application and they must be reviewed for expedited SNAP and ongoing SNAP.

NOTE: The Commonwealth of Pennsylvania Access to Social Services ( COMPASS ) Community Partners listing by Agency which is found in the CAO resources does not apply to this clarification. Those partners on that list may submit SNAP applications but would be required to still submit a signature page or e-sign the COMPASS

Accept and review each application for expedited service on the day the application is filed, even if there is not enough staffing or the office recieves a large number of applications.

Conduct the application interview for individuals who submit their application in person at the office or ensure that they have provided a phone number where they can be reached that day so they can be contacted for an interview.

For applications that are received through the mail, dropped off at the office or received via COMPASS, the CAO will attempt to contact the household on the day the application is received to conduct an interview .

NOTE: The CAO must interview the applicant prior to issuing expedited SNAP benefits

Frontload the application to the “SNAP Budget Authorization” screen.

NOTE: If the application is received at the end of the day or there are system issues and frontloading is not possible, complete a scratchpad and narrate the results of the review. Frontloading the application should be completed at the beginning of the next business day.

Process all applications for households eligible for expedited service so that benefits begin within 5 calendar days from the application filing date . Authorize expedited benefits no later than day 4 so benefits are available in the Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT) system by day 5.

Put up posters in the reception area explaining expedited service.

Have copies of PUB 52.1 , SNAP Benefits NOW in the reception area.

Updated March 12, 2026, replacing January 16, 2014
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.2 Application Filing 1
heading: 506.2 CAO Responsibilities at Application

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.2 Application Filing

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.3 Determining Entitlement to Expedited Service
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.3 Determining Entitlement to Expedited Service

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.3 Determining Entitlement to Expedited Service

body:
A household is entitled to expedited service if it meets any of the following criteria in the month of application:

All members are migrant or seasonal farm workers who are destitute as defined in Chapter 516, Migrant or Seasonal Farm Workers, have $100 or less in liquid resources and nothing else to live on.

NOTE: The CAO must use actual income received or expected to be received in the month to determine entitlement.

The value of total liquid resources is $100 or less, and countable monthly gross income is less than $150.

The household’s combined monthly gross income and liquid resources are less than its monthly shelter expenses. Shelter expenses include rent or mortgage, property taxes, homeowner's insurance, and the appropriate Standard Utility Allowance (SUA) based on the obligation to pay utility bills.

When determining entitlement, the County Assitance Office (CAO) must apply the appropriate SUA. To know what SUA is appropriate, the CAO must consider whether the household:

Incurs a heating or cooling cost,

Received assistance under the Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP) and has an elderly (age 60 or older) or disabled household member.

Receives the Heat and Eat (H&E) Benefit or is going to receive the Heat and Eat Benefit.

NOTE: A household must have an elderly or disabled household member to be eligible to receive the H&E benefit.

7 CFR § 273.2(i)1)

Updated March 12, 2016, replacing July 3, 2014
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.3 Determining Entitlement to Expedited Service 1
heading: 506.3 Determining Entitlement to Expedited Service

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.3 Determining Entitlement to Expedited Service

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.4 Time Frame for Issuing Benefits
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.4 Time Frame for Issuing Benefits

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.4 Time Frame for Issuing Benefits

body:
If a household meets any one of the criteria for expedited service and is eligible for benefits, the County Assistance Office (CAO) must make sure the first benefit is available within five calendar days.

7 CFR § 273.2(i)3)(i)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.4 Time Frame for Issuing Benefits 1
heading: 506.4 Time Frame for Issuing Benefits

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.4 Time Frame for Issuing Benefits

body:
The 5-day count begins the day after the application is filed and runs for 5 days in a row. Weekends and holidays are part of the 5-day count.

The CAO must issue the PA Electronic Benefit Transfer ( EBT) ( Also known as the ACCESS card ) and authorize benefits on or before the 4th calendar day after the application filing day, so that benefits are available on the EBT system on day 5.

The EBT system links the new EBT card and account overnight.

If the 5-day count begins on Wednesday, the CAO must issue the PA EBT card on Thursday or Friday to meet the deadline.

If the1st day of the 5-day count is followed by 4 nonbusiness days, the CAO must issue the card on day 1 to meet the 5-day deadline.

Example : Mr. Davis files an application for Supplemental Assistance Nutrition Program (SNAP) benefits on the Tuesday before Thanksgiving. He qualifies for expedited service. The 5-day count begins Wednesday. The CAO issues the PA EBT card no later than Wednesday, because the CAO is closed Thursday and Friday for the Thanksgiving holiday.

If the CAO does a telephone interview with a household and mails the application to the household for signature, mailing time is not part of the 5-day expedited deadline. Mailing time includes the days the application is in the mail to and from the household and the days the household has the application.

7 CFR § 273.2(i)3)(iii)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.4 Time Frame for Issuing Benefits 2
heading: 506.41 The Five-Day Expedited Deadline

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.4 Time Frame for Issuing Benefits

body:
If the preliminary review does not show that a household is entitled to expedited service and then the CAO discovers that the household is entitled, the 5-day count begins with the date the CAO discovers the entitlement.

7 CFR § 273.2(i)3)(iv)

If the CAO determines that a household is ineligible for expedited SNAP after reviewing its SNAP application, the CAO must send a Notice of Denial of Expedited Service PA/FS 163 and Pending Verification Letter PA 253 to the household. If the subsequent application interview indicates that the household does qualify for expedited SNAP, the 5-day time frame for authorizing benefits begins with the date of the interview.

Example: Mr. and Mrs. Green apply for SNAP benefits for themselves and 2 children on March 5th. Based on the income reported on the application form, the family is ineligible for expedited services. The caseworker mails a PA/FS 163 and PA 253 to the family scheduling a telephone interview for March 14th. The CAO completes the interview and discovers that the household no longer has earned income as previously reported. Based upon this new information, the family is eligible for expedited SNAP. The CAO must authorize benefits so that they are available on or before March 19th.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.4 Time Frame for Issuing Benefits 3
heading: 506.42 Delayed Expedited SNAP Processing

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.4 Time Frame for Issuing Benefits

body:
The CAO must not delay issuing expedited benefits to households applying for other programs. The CAO may schedule interviews for the other programs within the 5-day expedited time frame. If the household remains eligible for SNAP benefits,it must get the first benefit in EBT no later than the 5th calendar day.

7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)

Example: A household applies for both cash assistance and SNAP on Monday. The preliminary review shows the household is entitled to expedited service. The CAO must authorize SNAP benefits no later than the close of business on Friday. The CAO schedules an interview for cash assistance on Wednesday and delays the SNAP authorization while awaiting the outcome of the cash assistance interview. If the household misses the cash assistance appointment, the CAO still has enough time to meet the Friday expedited service deadline.

NOTE: The CAO must not delay processing expedited benefits while waiting for verification needed to decide the household's eligibility for another program.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.4 Time Frame for Issuing Benefits 4
heading: 506.43 Multiple Program Applications

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.4 Time Frame for Issuing Benefits

body:
Individuals receiving or applying for Supplemental Security Income ( SSI ) benefits may apply for SNAP benefits at the Social Security Administration (SSA) office.

The SSA will do the following:

Review all SNAP applications for expedited service on the day it receives the application.

Mark "EXPEDITED PROCESSING" on the PA 600 for all households meeting expedited criteria and tell the applicant that benefits may be available sooner if the household applies directly at the CAO.

7 CFR § 273.2(k)(1)(i)(I)

Verify identity.

Refer non-SSI households that want to apply for SNAP benefits to the correct CAO.

7 CFR § 273.2(k)(1)(i)(H)

The CAO must do the following:

Review all applications from the SSA for expedited service on the day it receives the application. The CAO is not required to do a face-to-face interview.

7 CFR § 273.2(k)(1)(i)(J)

Begin the five-day time frame for authorizing benefits on the date it receives the application.

7 CFR § 273.2(k)(1)(i)(F)

Updated March 12, 2026, replacing July 3, 2014
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.4 Time Frame for Issuing Benefits 5
heading: 506.44 Applications Taken at the Social Security Administration Office

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.4 Time Frame for Issuing Benefits

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.5 Denials
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.5 Denials

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.5 Denials

body:
The County Assitance Office ( CAO) must follow these steps on the day it decides that a household is not entitled to expedited service :

Tell the household that the expedited service is denied and give or send a Notice of Denial of Expedited Service ( PA/FS 163 ).

Tell the household that it has the right to meet with a supervisor within two work days.

Mail the ( PA/FS 163 ) to the household when the household is interviewed by telephone. This notice does not take the place of the Notice of Ineligibility ( PA/FS 162 ) telling applicants they are not eligible for Supplemantal Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) benefits.

Screen the application and schedule an interview using normal application processing procedures.

Add the following information to the case record:

Why expedited service was denied

How the household was told of the denial

How the household was told of its right to an agency conference

7 CFR § 273.15(d )

Updated March 12, 2026, replacing July 3, 2014
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.5 Denials 1
heading: 506.5 Denials

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.5 Denials

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

body: Meeting expedited service criteria does not mean that the household is eligible for benefits. A household may be ineligible for benefits because it did not meet other requirements.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits 1
heading: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

body:
The County Assistance Office (CAO) must complete an interview for households that submit an application in person and meet expedited service criteria. At this interview, the CAO must verify identity, determine eligibility and take the necessary steps to issue an Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) card (if the applicant does not have one) while the individual is in the office. If the individual can not stay for the interview, CAO personnel must ensure that the applicant leaves a number where they can be reached that day. The CAO should explain the expedited benefits cannot be issued until an interview is held.

If an application is dropped off or submitted online, by mail or fax and includes a telephone number, the CAO must attempt to contact the household that day. If the CAO reaches the applicant, it must conduct the telephone interview and process the application within the 5-day expedited service time frame. The CAO must determine eligibility by day 4 to meet the 5-day expedited service time frame.

The first day of this count is the calendar day after the filing date.

This interview serves as the application interview for ongoing Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) benefits.

The CAO must inform the applicant interviewed by telephone that they can come to the CAO to get an EBT card or ask the applicant if they would like the card locally mailed to them. Only issue an EBT card if the individual does not already have one.

Refer to the Desk Guide in Appendix B for additional clarification/guidance on the expedited interview process .
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits 2
heading: 506.61 The Interview

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

body:
The CAO must always verify the identity of the applicant , there are no exceptions to this rule. If there is a pending Out of State DX 15 exchange hit, the hit must be resolved before the expedited SNAP can be processed. The household can provide verification that they are no longer receiving benefits in the other state.

A reasonable effort must be made to verify other eligibility factors, but only if doing so will not delay benefits. The applicant must provide all postponed verification before the second month's benefits.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits 3
heading: 506.62 Verification

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

body:
If the applicant cannot provide documents to prove identity, the CAO must verify it through at least one collateral contact . In some situations, the CAO may require additional contacts. The CAO must help the applicant verify identity and document those efforts in the case record.

Examples:

The CAO contacts a landlord or someone named by the applicant, but that individual cannot verify the applicant’s identity. This single contact is enough, as long as the CAO records the effort in the case record.

The CAO calls a collateral contact, but there is no answer or the line is busy. This single attempt is not enough to be considered a "reasonable effort," and the CAO must make more attempts.

When an applicant mails the application form or leaves it at the local office, the CAO must try to call the applicant to verify their identity. If the CAO cannot reach the applicant by phone, it:

Must check Department of Human Services (DHS) records for proof of identity. If the household:

Receives other benefits from DHS, identity is deemed verified and does not need to be re-verified.

Received benefits from DHS in the past, identity does not have to be re-verified.

Applied for DHS benefits in the past and submitted verification of identity with that application, the CAO does not need to request additional verification.

May use Social Security Administration (SSA) data if the applicant is unknown to DHS. SSA has established an interface to verify the citizenship and identity of individuals declaring U.S. citizenship. If there is a positive match for U.S. citizenship, MCI and e CIS will update with an electronic verification code (V) and a source code for SSA (S).

Identity verified by Remote Identity Proofing (RIDP) during the online application process is considered sufficient verification.

There is no quality control error on an expedited issuance case if the decision to provide expedited service is correct based on information written on the application and in the case record and identity is properly verified.

7 CFR § 275.12(d)2)(ii)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits 4
heading: 506.621 Verification of Identity

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

body:
If the CAO cannot verify identity, the CAO must not authorize benefits until it receives verification. The CAO must not reject the application, but it must follow normal rules for processing applications, including the requirement to schedule a second interview. See Chapter 504 .

If the applicant provides verification of identity within 30 days of the filing date and is found eligible, the CAO must authorize benefits on the same day that identity is verified or on the following work day.

If the household fails to comply by the 30th day, the CAO must deny the application. See Chapter 504 for information on processing delays beyond 30 days.

A reasonable effort must be made to verify other eligibility factors, but only if doing so will not delay benefits. The applicant must provide all postponed verification before the second month's benefits.

There is no quality control error on an expedited issuance case if the decision to provide expedited service is correct based on information written on the application and in the case record and identity is properly verified.

7 CFR § 275.12(d)2)(ii)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits 5
heading: 506.622 Failure to Verify Identity

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

body:
Households must provide a Social Security Number (SSN) for each member or apply for an SSN before the first full month of benefits following authorization of expedited SNAP benefits.

7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)(i)(B )

The CAO must refer a household to the local ( SSA ) if a member does not have or cannot verify an SSN. (See Supplemental Handbook, Chapter 950 .) This applies to any household member who does not have or cannot verify an SSN. The CAO must not delay benefits.

NOTE: If the applicant cannot provide an SSN within the time frame, see the good cause rules in Supplemental Handbook, Chapter 950 .
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits 6
heading: 506.63 Enumeration

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

body:
The CAO must postpone enrollment in employment activities if enrollment cannot be completed within the required time frame for issuing expedited benefits.

A household is not eligible for benefits, including expedited benefits, during any period when the household is disqualified because the principal wage earner does not meet work requirements or quits a job voluntarily.

If a household applies during the last month of a sanction period, expedited requirements apply to the first month of eligibility after the sanction period.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits 7
heading: 506.64 Enrollment in the Employment and Training Program

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

body: The CAO must count only liquid resources when determining entitlement to expedited service. It must count both liquid and nonliquid resources when determining eligibility for ongoing benefits.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits 8
heading: 506.65 Resources

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

body:
The household must give resource information (not verification) about its resources on the application or during the interview.

If the resource information is not included on a mailed application, during the interview attempted on day 0 the caseworker should try to gather this information. If the CAO cannot reach the household by phone, the CAO must mail a SNAP Interview Notice (PA 1599) on the day it receives the application.

NOTE: The system will evaluate the household as not eligible for expedited, but will reevaluate when the interview is completed and update the notice, automated narrative and information on the application details screen in AP.

NOTE: The CAO must make every effort to get the information in time to meet the expedited time frame for issuing SNAP benefits and must deny the application if the household does not provide the information by the 30th day.

The CAO must record in the case record all efforts to assist the household.

EXCEPTION: Resource information is not required if the CAO determines that the household is ineligible for expedited SNAP benefits based on reported income. The CAO should answer “No” to questions 3 and 7 on the expedited SNAP questions page (CAPXFS) so the PA 1599 is not sent to the household.

The CAO sends a Pending Verification Letter PA 253 to schedule an interview for ongoing SNAP benefits .
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits 9
heading: 506.651 Resource Information

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

body:
All income requirements apply, including deeming the income of ineligible household members.

7 CFR § 273.9(b)

While past income may predict future income, the CAO must count only income that the household reasonably expects to get. The CAO must not count income for which the receipt date or amount is uncertain.

7 CFR § 273.10(c)(1)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits 10
heading: 506.66 Income

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

body:
For joint cash assistance and SNAP applications, the CAO must count the cash benefit only if it will definitely be received during the month of application. Since cash authorizations are available the next day on EBT, the CAO must count all cash benefits authorized on or before the next to the last day of the month. The CAO must determine the cash authorization within the expedited time frames to count it in the expedited SNAP calculation.

Example : The CAO receives an application for cash assistance and SNAP benefits on March 10. The household meets the criteria for expedited service and provides all verification and information on the 10th. The interview for cash assistance is scheduled for March 16. Since eligibility for cash is not yet certain, the CAO does not count the cash benefit as income.

If the household will definitely receive a cash assistance benefit in the application month, it may still be entitled to expedited service if total income including the cash assistance benefit is less than $150.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits 11
heading: 506.661 Joint Cash Assistance and SNAP Applications

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

body:
The household must give income information on the application or during the interview before the CAO can determine whether the household is eligible for expedited benefits.

To determine entitlement, the CAO must use actual income received or expected to be received in the month. To determine benefit amount, the CAO must follow procedures outlined in Section 567.2 .

If income information is not included on a mailed application, the CAO must call the household immediately. If the CAO cannot reach the household by phone, the CAO must mail a PA 1599 form on the day it receives the application so that the household can respond within the expedited time frame.

The CAO must make every effort to get the information in time to meet the expedited time frame and must deny the application if the household does not provide the information by the 30th day.

The CAO must record in the case record all efforts to assist the household.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits 12
heading: 506.662 Income Information

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

body:
If the applicant has utility costs, the CAO must use the appropriate standard utility allowance (SUA) when deciding whether the household is entitled to expedited service. (See Chapter 560.831 .)

7 CFR § 273.9 (d)(6)(iii)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits 13
heading: 506.67 Shelter Expenses

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

body:
It is not an overpayment if the CAO later verifies that income or resources in the application month were above expedited or ongoing eligibility limits or should have resulted in a lower benefit amount unless the household misrepresented information on the application or during the interview.

7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)

It is not an overpayment if the household gets a cash assistance benefit that the CAO did not expect when figuring out the amount of the expedited allotment.

Updated March 12, 2026, replacing March 28, 2024
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits 14
heading: 506.68 Overissuances

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.6 Determining Eligibility for Expedited Benefits

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.7 Certification Periods
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.7 Certification Periods

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.7 Certification Periods

body:
The County Assitance Office ( CAO) must assign a certification period according to household circumstances. A household may be certified for one or two months only if the CAO cannot reasonably predict future circumstances or if the household was eligible for expedited benefits for one or two months.

55 Pa. Code § 501.9(f)

Example: A household applies for both cash assistance and SNAP benefits on April 28 and is entitled to expedited service . An interview for cash assistance is scheduled for May 5. The household provides all required verification. The CAO authorizes SNAP benefits on the 28th. Because the household applied after the 15th, the CAO authorizes SNAP benefits for May. The CAO does not count the cash assistance benefit when figuring out May's SNAP benefit. The CAO certifies the household for a normal period, because the outcome of the cash assistance interview can be handled as a change for June.

NOTE: The expedited and ongoing certification periods do not need to add up to 12 months as they are considered separate certification periods. The ongoing certification period begins the month the ongoing benefits are issued.

If the CAO interviews the applicant and postpones verification, the CAO must take the following actions:

Establish a certification period according to the household's circumstances.

Authorize noncontinuous eligibility (NCE) for the application month.

Send PA 253 with details of what needs to be verified prior to authorizing benefits.

Deny benefits for the second month if postponed verification is not provided within 30 days of the date of application or there is no interview.

Authorize benefits for the second and following months after receiving all postponed verifications.

Enter the renewal due date according to the certification period established at application, without requiring a new application.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.7 Certification Periods 1
heading: 506.7 Certification Periods

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.7 Certification Periods

body:
There is no limit to the number of times a household may be certified under expedited procedures. However, before each expedited certification, the household must:

7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)(iv)

Provide the verification postponed at the previous expedited certification; or

Have been certified under normal processing standards since that time.

Households whose benefits stop within a certification period are ineligible for expedited service if they reapply before the certification period ends. If a household submits an untimely Benefits Review Form ( PA 600R ) after a certification period ends, the CAO must review for expedited service.

7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)(v)

Updated March 12, 2026, replacing October 3, 2014
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.7 Certification Periods 2
heading: 506.71 Limitations on Expedited Certification

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.7 Certification Periods

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.8 Expedited Issuance Procedures
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.8 Expedited Issuance Procedures

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.8 Expedited Issuance Procedures

body:
PFS17947506 Processing Expedited SNAP for ABAWD's March 30, 2016

The expedited criteria in Section 506.3 apply only at the initial determination of eligibility for expedited service . Eligible households applying for initial benefits after the 15th of the month that are entitled to expedited processing must receive a combined allotment consisting of prorated benefits for the initial month and full benefits for the second month.

7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)(iii)(C)

Households applying after the 15th of the month are entitled to expedited benefits for the second month, even if the initial benefit is prorated to zero.

Regardless of the application date, the length of the certification period, or the existence of postponed verification, the CAO must do the following:

7 CFR § 273.2(i)(2)

Complete a system inquiry to determine if any household members are active recipients or are disqualified.

Determine if there are postponed verifications from a previous expedited benefit.

Explain to the applicant that expedited service is available.

7 CFR § 273.2(i)(3)(i)

Explain that benefits will be issued electronically through Electronic Benefit Transfer ( EBT) and that the recipient must go to the CAO to get the PA EBT card and choose a Personal Identification Number ( PIN) .

Determine eligibility and, if the household is eligible, authorize benefits.

Make sure the date the application was received is entered in eCIS. Override the system date if it is not the actual date of application.

Authorize the first expedited benefit using the regular opening process with an One Time Issuance ( OTI) if all verification is provided or using the Noncontinuous Eligibility ( NCE) process if verification is postponed.

Explain in the case notes any processing delay.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.8 Expedited Issuance Procedures 1
heading: 506.8 Expedited Issuance Procedures

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.8 Expedited Issuance Procedures

body:
If the household provides all verification when applying or within the 5-day expedited service time limit and is eligible, the CAO must process a regular opening with a centrally issued OTI for the first month.

If there is postponed verification, the CAO must process a centrally issued NCE for the first month.

When the household provides all required postponed verifications, the CAO must:

Determine eligibility for continued benefits; and

Process a regular opening for the second month, if the household is eligible.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.8 Expedited Issuance Procedures 2
heading: 506.81 Issuing Benefits for Households Applying on or before the 15th of the Month

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.8 Expedited Issuance Procedures

body:
If the household provides all required verification within the 5 -day time limit and is eligible, the CAO must process a regular opening with a centrally issued OTI for the first and second months.

If verification is postponed, the CAO must process a locally issued NCE for the first and second months.

When the household provides all required postponed verifications, the CAO must:

Determine eligibility for continued benefits; and

Process a regular opening for the third month, if the household is eligible.

This opening cannot be processed until after the deadline for the second month of the NCE period has passed.

7 CFR 273.2(i)(4)(iii)

Updated March 12, 2026 Replacing April 14, 2016
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.8 Expedited Issuance Procedures 3
heading: 506.82 Issuing Benefits for Households Applying after the 15th of the Month

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 506.8 Expedited Issuance Procedures

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Appendix B: Expedited SNAP Interview
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Appendix B: Expedited SNAP Interview

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Appendix B: Expedited SNAP Interview

body:
Expedited Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program ( SNAP ) Interview Desk Guide

Walk-In Applications Eligible for Expedited SNAP Benefits Ineligible for Expedited SNAP Benefits STEPS: • Conduct the application interview and provide the applicant with a list of verification items necessary to receive ongoing benefits. • Authorize and issue expedited SNAP benefits within the five-day time frame but no later than day 4. • Send the Notice to Applicant (PA/FS 162) , informing applicant of eligibility for expedited SNAP. STEPS: • Conduct the application interview and provide the applicant with a list of verification items necessary to receive ongoing benefits. • Provide the applicant with the Notice of Denial of Expedited Services ( PA/FS 163 ) at the time of the interview informing the applicant of their ineligibility for expedited SNAP benefits. Mail-In, Drop-Off, Fax or COMPASS Applications- Telephone Interview Completed E ligible for Expedited SNAP Benefits Ineligible for Expedited SNAP Benefits STEPS: • Attempt to contact the applicant by telephone on the day the application is received to conduct an expedited SNAP interview. • Conduct an application interview at the time of contact and provide the applicant with a list of verification items necessary to receive ongoing benefits. • Authorize expedited SNAP benefits no later than day 4 . • Send PA/FS 162 , informing applicant of eligibility for expedited SNAP . .• Narrate in the case record narrative how the applicant was informed of their eligibility for expedited SNAP benefits. STEPS: • Conduct the application interview and provide the applicant with a list of verification items necessary to receive ongoing benefits. • Send a PA/FS 163 informing the applicant of their ineligibility for expedited SNAP benefits. • Narrate in the case record narrative how the applicant was informed of their ineligibility for expedited SNAP benefits. Mail-In, Drop-Off, Fax or COMPASS Applications- Interview Not Completed STEPS : • Attempt to contact the applicant by telephone on the day the application is received to conduct an expedited SNAP interview. • If unable to contact the applicant, send a SNAP Interview Notice ( PA 1599 ) and a Pending Verification Form ( PA 253 ) on the day the application is received (when operationally possible). The PA 1599 will tell the applicant that they must be interviewed to determine their eligibility for Quick SNAP. The PA 253 will inform the applicant about their need to complete an interview and any other pending information that may be required to process ongoing benefits. NOTE: The household does not need to do two interviews. An interview completed during the expedited time frame serves as the application interview for ongoing SNAP benefits. • If the applicant does not respond to the PA 1599 and PA 253, see below. F ailure to respond to the PA 1599 and PA 253 • If the household responds, is interviewed and is eligible, authorize expedited SNAP benefits or send a PA/FS 163 if the household is ineligible for expedited SNAP for another reason. This may include income/resources greater than shelter costs, identity, or failure to provide pending information from a previous expedited issuance. • If the applicant fails to respond to the PA 1599 and PA 253 , send a Notice of Missed Interview (NOMI) to notify the household of the missed interview and its responsibility to contact the CAO to set up another interview. The NOMI should be sent based on the date on the PA 253. •If the applicant does not respond to the NOMI or complete the interview by day 30, send a PA/FS 162 Notice of Ineligibility and reject the application using code 047. • Narrate actions taken and eligibility decision in the case record.

Updated March 12, 2026, Replacing March 28, 2024
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Appendix B: Expedited SNAP Interview 1
heading: Appendix B: Expedited SNAP Interview Desk Guide
