citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 570: Reporting Changes
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 570: Reporting Changes

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 570: Reporting Changes

body:
Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Handbook

Chapter 570: Reporting Changes
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 570: Reporting Changes 1
heading: Chapter 579 Reopening Benefits

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 570: Reporting Changes

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.1 General Policy
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.1 General Policy

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.1 General Policy

body:
National Accuracy Clearinghouse (NAC) Exchange Hit Guidance, PFS22480570 (Published April 24, 2026)

This chapter applies to households enrolled in the semiannual reporting (SAR) system as well as to households not enrolled in SAR.

The CAO must advise a household , in a language the household members understand, of their responsibility to report certain changes in circumstances on time.

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(2 )

Exception: Households receiving Extended SNAP benefits have no reporting requirements during the Extended SNAP period.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.1 General Policy 1
heading: 570.1 General Policy

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.1 General Policy

body:
An applicant household must provide all information related to SNAP eligibility and benefit amount at the certification interview. If a change occurs after the interview but before the Notice to Applicant is issued, the household must report that change within 10 days of the notice date.

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(3)

A participating non-SAR household must report a change in circumstances by the 10th day following the month of change.

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(2)

A participating SAR household must report income that exceeds 130 percent of the federal poverty income guidelines (FPIG) by the 10th day following the month of change.

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(i)(vii)

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(i)(B)(viii)

NOTE: If the household contains an able-bodied adult without dependents (ABAWD), the ABAWD must report work hours that fall below 80 hours per month by the 10th day following the month of change.

If a SAR or NON-SAR household voluntarily reports a change, the CAO must act on it for both increases and decreases in benefits.

7 CFR § 273.12(a)

If a SNAP household receives cash assistance or Medical Assistance and reports a change, the change is considered reported for SNAP purposes. (See Section 570.5 .)

7 CFR § 273.12(f)

NOTE: The procedure for changing the amount of the benefit because of a reported change is explained in Chapter 568 .

Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing March 20, 2008
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.1 General Policy 2
heading: 570.11 Timely Reporting

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.1 General Policy

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.2 Types of Changes to Be Reported
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.2 Types of Changes to Be Reported

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.2 Types of Changes to Be Reported

body: Types of changes to be reported depend on if the household is enrolled in SAR .
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.2 Types of Changes to Be Reported 1
heading: 570.2 Types of Changes to Be Reported

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.2 Types of Changes to Be Reported

body:
A household enrolled in SAR must report:

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(i)(vii)

Gross monthly income exceeding 130 percent of the federal poverty income guidelines (FPIG); and

Work hours that fall below 80 hours per month, or an average of 20 hours weekly for a household that contains an ABAWD .

NOTE: A reported change affecting cash assistance or Medical Assistance benefits also affects SNAP benefits.

When an individual moves into a household, it is the household’s responsibility to add the new person’s income to any other household income when determining if the total household income exceeds 130% of the FPIG. The household is required to compare the total household income to the original 130% FPIG limit given to the household.

If a household reports that a new person has moved into the household, it is the CAO’S responsibility to determine if the new member is a mandatory household member and to add the person and his or her income. (See Section 568.6 , and Section 568.7 .)

Examples:

Mrs. Smith and her three children receive SNAP benefits only. Mrs. Smith’s 130% FPIG reporting requirement is for a family of four. Mr. Smith, who has income, moves back into the household on September 1. Mrs. Smith counts all of the household’s gross monthly income, including her husband’s, and compares that amount with the FPIG limit for a family of four. On October 6, Mrs. Smith reports and verifies that her husband returned to the household and that the household income exceeds the FPIG limit for a family of four for September. The case worker determines that Mr. Smith is a mandatory household member. Mr. Smith and his income are added to the SNAP benefit. The 130% FPIG for the Smiths will be for a family of five.

Mr. and Mrs. Wilson and their three children receive SNAP benefits. Their FPIG limit is for a family of five. Mr. and Mrs. Wilson are both working part-time. Mr. Wilson moves out of the household. Mrs. Wilson does not report this change. To determine the household’s gross monthly income, Mrs. Wilson totals the remaining household members’ income and compares this income amount with the FPIG limit for a family of five. Because Mr. Wilson left the household, Mrs. Wilson does not have to figure out his monthly income for the 130% FPIG comparison. The SNAP benefit and the 130% FPIG limit will remain the same until Mrs. Wilson reports the change at the six-month review or renewal , whichever comes first.

NOTE: Mr. Wilson must be removed from Mrs. Wilson’s SNAP benefits if he applies for SNAP benefits for himself. Persons may not receive SNAP benefits in more than one household.

Mr. and Mrs. Nguyen and their three children receive SNAP benefits. Their FPIG limit is for a family of five. Mr. and Mrs. Nguyen are both working part-time. Mr. Nguyen leaves the household on September 6, and Mrs. Nguyen calls to report this on September 10. The case worker increases benefits for October and provides Mrs. Nguyen the FPIG amount for a family of four.

If a SAR household reports a change of address, the CAO must verify the new address at the next SAR review or renewal. SNAP benefits will be adjusted based on the new shelter and utility costs.

NOTE: If the client reports a change of address, offer the client the opportunity to register to vote or change the registration address. Provide the voter registration form if the client wishes to register to vote or change the registration address, and assist the client in completing the registration form if the client requests help. (See Supplemental Handbook, Chapter 980 .)

42 U.S.C.A. § 1973 gg-5 (a)(2)(A)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.2 Types of Changes to Be Reported 2
heading: 570.21 SAR Household Reporting Requirements

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.2 Types of Changes to Be Reported

body:
A household not enrolled in SAR must report changes to the following:

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(1)

Income and the source of income, regardless of the amount received

Unearned income over $125

Who lives in the household

Address and new shelter costs

NOTE: If the client reports a change of address, offer the client the opportunity to register to vote or change the registration address. Provide the voter registration form if the client wishes to register to vote or change the registration address, and assist the client in completing the registration form if the client requests help . (See Supplemental Handbook, Chapter 980 .)

42 U.S.C.A. § 1973 gg-5 (a)(2)(A)

Ownership of licensed vehicle

Cash on hand, bank accounts, stocks, or bonds that reach or exceed the eligibility limit

The legal obligation to pay child support

Reminder: Extended SNAP households do not have to report changes during the Extended SNAP period.

7 CFR § 273.12(f)(4)(ii)

NOTE: If the Extended SNAP household reports a change that increases the SNAP benefit, the household must complete the renewal process before benefits may increase.

Elderly and disabled household members do not have to report changes in medical expenses during the certification period .

7 CFR § 273.10(d)

Updated September 6, 2022, replacing October 25, 2017
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.2 Types of Changes to Be Reported 3
heading: 570.22 Non-SAR Household Reporting Requirements

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.2 Types of Changes to Be Reported

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.3 Reporting Methods
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.3 Reporting Methods

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.3 Reporting Methods

body:
A change report form ( PA 239-SP ) is used to report changes. A household may also report by phone, by mail, by fax, electronically, or through someone acting on the household's behalf. The CAO must give the household a local number that accepts collect calls or an 800 number for reporting changes by phone.

7 CFR § 273.12(b)(1)

7 CFR § 273.12(b)(3)

The CAO must provide change reporting household s with a PA 239-SP and a self-addressed postage paid envelope:

7 CFR § 273.12(b)(4)

At certification (this is sent automatically by the system to the appropriate households)

At renewal ; and

When a household returns a PA 239-SP .

The CAO must keep the PA 239-SP in the case record for three years.

Updated September 6, 2022 , replacing March 1, 2012
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.3 Reporting Methods 1
heading: 570.3 Reporting Methods

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.3 Reporting Methods

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.4 Verification
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.4 Verification

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.4 Verification

body:
When a household reports a change listed in Section 570.2 , or when the CAO learns of a change through a “verified upon receipt” source (see Section 570.421 ) , the CAO must verify the change according to the requirements in Chapter 578 and Appendix A of this chapter.

7 CFR § 273.12(c)

Benefit increases or decreases will be made after the household provides verification. The household must provide verification within 10 days of the date of the reported change.

The CAO will send a written request for clarification reminding the household to verify the reported change. The request must include the date when the information must be received by the CAO.

7 CFR § 273.12(c)(3)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.4 Verification 1
heading: 570.4 Verification

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.4 Verification

body:
Changes resulting in a benefit increase must be verified according to Chapter 578 .

All households must verify the change before the CAO increases SNAP benefits. If the household reports the change but does not verify it, the SNAP benefit must remain at the current level. If the household fails to report and verify a change that increases SNAP benefits on time, the CAO must not authorize a corrective payment .

7 CFR § 273.12(c)(1)(iii)

NOTE: A household is not required to verify total medical expenses if they are unchanged or have changed by $25 or less. This applies unless the information is incomplete, inaccurate, inconsistent, or outdated.

If a SNAP household fails to verify a deductible expense, SNAP benefits are determined without the deduction. SNAP benefits are continued if the household is eligible without the deduction.

When a household reports and verifies a change that results in a benefit increase, the CAO has 10 days to make the benefit adjustment. However, the CAO will process the change as soon as possible.

7 CFR § 273.12(C)

NOTE: The benefit must be increased no later than the month following the month the household reported and verified the change. If the deadline cannot be met to change the benefits, the CAO will issue a supplementary benefit.

7 CFR § 273.12(C)(1)(ii)

Example: A household reports and verifies a decrease in income at the end of May. It is too late for the CAO to adjust the SNAP benefit for the issuance date of June 1. The CAO must issue a supplementary benefit for June.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.4 Verification 2
heading: 570.41 Verifying Changes that Result in a Benefit Increase

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.4 Verification

body:
Changes resulting in a benefit decrease are subject to the verification requirements in Chapter 578 .

7 CFR § 273.12(c)(2)

If a SAR household reports a change in Section 570.21 or any other change not required to be reported, or if a non-SAR household reports a change in Section 570.22 or any other change that will result in a benefit decrease, the CAO will:

Send a Request for Clarification/Verification (contact letter).

7 CFR § 273.12(c)(3)(i)

If the verification is received in a timely manner, reduce or terminate the household’s benefits and send an Advance Notice of Adverse Action .

If the verification is not received, terminate the household’s benefits and send an Advance Notice of Adverse Action.

Exception: If an individual moves out of the household, the CAO does not need to verify the person's absence unless it is questionable.

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(x)

NOTE: If an Extended SNAP household reports a change that would decrease benefits, the CAO will not reduce benefits.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.4 Verification 3
heading: 570.42 Verifying Changes that Result in a Benefit Decrease

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.4 Verification

body:
The following sources are considered “verified upon receipt” and do not require additional information before the CAO takes action:

IEVS

Exchange #2 ( UC )

Exchange #3 ( BENDEX )

Exchange #6 ( SDX )

Exchange #7 ( buy-in )

SAVE

For SAR or non-SAR households, the CAO must take the following actions for those sources considered “verified upon receipt”:

Review eligibility based on the information provided by SAVE.

Review the information provided by IEVS.

Follow the procedures in Section 578.81 for the information provided by IEVS.

Narrate the actions taken in the case record.

NOTE: For SNAP-only or SNAP/Medical Assistance cases, regardless of participation in SAR, the CAO must average the UC benefits or SSI benefits that fluctuate over the SAR or certification period . If a match is received on exchanges #2 and #6 after the fluctuating UC or SSI is averaged and adjusted against the SNAP benefits, the CAO is not required to take action until the next six-month SAR review or renewal .

At the six-month SAR review or renewal, the CAO determines if UC or SSI that was averaged should be changed.

A match on exchanges #2 and #6 indicating new income requires the CAO to adjust this income against the SNAP benefits.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.4 Verification 4
heading: 570.421 Sources Verified Upon Receipt

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.4 Verification

body:
The following sources are considered reliable but require additional information before the CAO takes action:

IEVS

Exchange #1 (new hires/wages match)

Exchange #4 (earning reference)

Exchange #5 (unearned income)

Exchange #8 (deceased persons match)

Exchange #9 ( PARIS )

Exchange #10 ( CJIS )

For all IEVS exchanges, the CAO must follow procedures in Chapter 578 and Appendix A of this chapter.

PACSES

The CAO must average the amount of support being paid by a legally responsible relative (LRR) and adjust that against the SNAP benefits. The average amount of support being paid by the LRR must be reviewed again at the next six-month SAR review or renewal, whichever is first. If the average amount of support has changed, the CAO must adjust the new averaged amount of support for the next SAR or certification period.

OFFICIAL SOURCE

An official source is information provided by a state, local, or federal governmental agency, such as the Department of Corrections or County Children and Youth Agencies.

NOTE: When receiving information from an official source, the CAO will follow the same procedure as for changes reported by a household. (See Section 570.42 .)

Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing May 16, 2006
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.4 Verification 5
heading: 570.422 Reliable Sources

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.4 Verification

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.5 Cash Assistance and SNAP Households
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.5 Cash Assistance and SNAP Households

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.5 Cash Assistance and SNAP Households

body:
When a household receiving both cash assistance and SNAP benefits reports a change in circumstances during the SAR or certification period , the CAO must determine the effect of the change on both programs. The CAO will make changes to the SNAP benefit when changes are reported for cash assistance.

7 CFR § 273.12(f)(1)

The CAO will notify the household of changes in:

7 CFR § 273.12(f)(3)

SNAP benefits

Work registration requirements

The SAR period

The certification period
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.5 Cash Assistance and SNAP Households 1
heading: 570.5 Cash Assistance and SNAP Households

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.5 Cash Assistance and SNAP Households

body:
When cash assistance and SNAP benefits are reduced or terminated within the SAR or certification period, the CAO will send one Advance Notice of Adverse Action to notify the household of actions on both programs.

7 CFR § 273.12(f)(3)(i)(A)

7 CFR § 273.12(f)(3)

If the household does not appeal within the period shown on the Advance Notice of Adverse Action , the benefit is decreased. (See Chapter 568 .)

If the household appeals within the period shown on the Advance Notice of Adverse Action, the CAO must continue benefits in the current amount unless the household waives the right to continued benefits.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.5 Cash Assistance and SNAP Households 2
heading: 570.51 Reduced or Terminated Cash Assistance and SNAP Benefits

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.5 Cash Assistance and SNAP Households

body:
When cash assistance is reduced or terminated and SNAP benefits are increased, the CAO will send an Advance Notice of Adverse Action to notify the household of the change in the cash assistance only.

7 CFR § 273.12(f)(4)(ii)

The CAO will increase the SNAP benefit after the household decides whether it will appeal the change. If the time frame to appeal expires and the household has not asked for a fair hearing , increase the benefit.

NOTE: The CAO has 10 days to act on the change after the time period to appeal expires.

Reminder: The CAO must determine whether the household is eligible for Extended SNAP if cash assistance ends.

If the household appeals a cash assistance reduction or termination on time and the benefit remains unchanged, the SNAP benefit must continue at the level it was at before the proposed cash assistance adverse action.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.5 Cash Assistance and SNAP Households 3
heading: 570.52 Reduced or Terminated Cash Assistance and Increased SNAP Benefits

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.5 Cash Assistance and SNAP Households

body:
The CAO will not close a SNAP case because cash assistance is terminated. Eligibility for SNAP benefits is determined based on information available . If there is not enough information to determine SNAP eligibility or benefit level, the CAO will send a written request for clarification (contact letter) to the household at the same time the Confirming Notice or Advance Notice of Adverse Action is sent for cash assistance. The CAO must allow at least ten days for the household to respond to the written request for clarification before taking further action.

7 CFR § 273.12(c)(3)

7 CFR § 273.12(f)(5)

If the household does not request a fair hearing after an Advance Notice of Adverse Action for cash assistance is sent and does not respond to the written request for clarification, the CAO will take action after the time period expires for appeal and written request for clarification.

If the household requests a fair hearing after an Advance Notice of Adverse Action for cash assistance is sent, but does not respond to the written request for clarification, the CAO will continue the cash assistance and SNAP benefits at the same amount. After the appeal is settled or the household clarifies SNAP information, the CAO will adjust SNAP benefits and send an appropriate notice. If the household has not complied with the written request for clarification, the CAO must send an Advance Notice of Adverse Action to close the SNAP case.

7 CFR § 273.12(f)(5)(i)

If the household requests a fair hearing and cash assistance is continued pending the appeal, the household's SNAP benefits are continued at the same amount..

Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing May 3, 2007
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.5 Cash Assistance and SNAP Households 4
heading: 570.53 Cash Assistance is Terminated and Information Needed to Determine the SNAP Benefit is not Available

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.5 Cash Assistance and SNAP Households

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.6 Failure to Report
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.6 Failure to Report

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.6 Failure to Report

body:
If the CAO learns of a change that the household failed to report as required in Section 570.2 , and the household received benefits to which it was not entitled, the CAO will file a claim against the household.

If the CAO becomes aware of a change during the SAR or certification period, the CAO will:

Verify the change using language services where appropriate. (Review the procedures in Section 570.42 and Chapter 578 .)

Send an Advance Notice of Adverse Action , unless one of the exceptions to its use applies.

Reduce or terminate benefits.

Complete an Overpayment Referral Data Input form ( PA 189 ).

Exception: The CAO will complete the manual Overpayment Referral form ( OIG 189 ) to refer SNAP employment and training special allowance overpayments to the Office of Inspector General (see Supplemental Handbook Chapter 910.5 ).

NOTE: While failure to report a change may cause an overpayment for another program, a SNAP-SAR overpayment occurs only if income exceeds the 130% FPIG or if ABAWD work hours fall below an average of 20 hours per week.

7 CFR § 273.12(d)

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing March 20, 2008
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.6 Failure to Report 1
heading: 570.6 Failure to Report

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 570.6 Failure to Report

heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Appendix A: IEVS/SAVE/PACSES and SAR Desk Guide

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 571: Semi-Annual Reporting (SAR)
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 571: Semi-Annual Reporting (SAR)

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 571: Semi-Annual Reporting (SAR)

body:
Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Handbook

Chapter 571: Semi-Annual Reporting (SAR)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 571: Semi-Annual Reporting (SAR) 1
heading: 571 Title

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 571: Semi-Annual Reporting (SAR)

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.1 General Policy
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.1 General Policy

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.1 General Policy

body:
S NAP Homeless Household SAR code, PFS-20291-571 (Revised February 25, 2021; Published January 14, 2021)

FS 43 and FS 75 SARS , PFS-20207-571 (Published November 10, 2020)

Signature on SAR LIN, PFS-18905-571 (Published May 2, 2018)

As a condition of eligibility, households enrolled in semiannual reporting (SAR) must fill out and return a SAR form ( PA 564 ) by the end of the appropriate annual or semiannual reporting period.

The SAR form ( PA 564 ) is printed with data from the Client Information System (CIS). See Chapter 571, Appendix A .

The CAO must use the information from the SAR form to determine a household's eligibility and benefit amount for the next semiannual period.

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)

The SAR form is available in Spanish. The CAO will enter the appropriate code in CIS:

7 CFR § 273.12(b)(2)

1 —English

2 —Spanish

SAR forms must be kept in the case record for three years from the month they were mailed.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.1 General Policy 1
heading: 571.1 General Policy

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.1 General Policy

body:
The CAO must enroll all SNAP households in SAR, except those receiving PA CAP (FS70) , FS 75 or Extended SNAP (FS43) (extended benefits - see 570.1 ).

55 Pa Code § 501.10(a)

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)

NOTE: Households with all adult members that are elderly and/or disabled are enrolled in SAR.

NOTE: FS 75 households do not have a SAR requirement and are considered change reporting.

NOTE: Households certified for 24 months will report in the 12th month of the certification period.

Although an Extended SNAP household has no reporting requirement during the Extended SNAP period, the household may request the CAO to complete a renewal if circumstances change that could increase its SNAP benefit.

If the household is eligible for TANF and/or increased SNAP benefits, the CAO must:

Close Extended SNAP,

Open the appropriate category of assistance (TANF and/or SNAP)

Enroll the household in SAR, and

Inform the household of SAR reporting requirements. See SNAP handbook chapter 571.21 and Chapters 570, Reporting Changes and 577, Notices.

Example: Ms L is working and receives Extended SNAP. She reports that her niece and nephew recently moved in with her for an indefinite period. The CAO completes a renewal, recertifies the household and opens regular SNAP, enrolls the household in SAR and informs Ms L of SAR reporting requirements.

Updated February 16, 2023, replacing January 31, 2023
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.1 General Policy 2
heading: 571.11 Enrollment in Semiannual Reporting

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.1 General Policy

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.2 The Semiannual Reporting System
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.2 The Semiannual Reporting System

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.2 The Semiannual Reporting System

body:
The Cash Assistance/SNAP Semiannual Reporting System (CFSAR) controls the benefit issuance to SAR households. CFSAR issues the SAR form to each SAR household according to the case-issuance cycle. The CAO must enter information into CFSAR to show whether the SAR form is complete or incomplete. Updating of the system is called tracking .

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)

If the SAR form is not completed and tracked by the last tracking day (see Chapter 571, Appendix C ), CFSAR will suspend benefits and send a SAR Late/Incomplete Notice (L/IN) to the household

The L/IN shows what information is needed and informs the household that the L/IN must be returned no later than the extended due date.

The CAO must process a reinstate-case action to issue a benefit after a case is suspended and beyond the last day for system override.

The CAO must review and close a suspended case if it is not reinstated.

The CAO must terminate benefits if the household fails to comply with SAR requirements during the suspension month.

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(iii)(D)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.2 The Semiannual Reporting System 1
heading: 571.2 The Semiannual Reporting System

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.2 The Semiannual Reporting System

body:
The CAO must notify households of the SAR reporting requirement, including the consequences of failure to file a SAR form, at application and renewal .

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(ii)

Before enrolling the household in SAR, the CAO must take the following actions:

Explain verbally and in writing how SAR works

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(ii)(D)

Give the household a copy of the SAR form and explain how to complete and return the form.

Give the household information about SNAP income limits, Client Message (CM 430)(see Chapter 577, Appendix B ).

Explain that only the following changes must be reported during the SAR period, by the 10th of the month after the month of change (see Chapter 570 ):

Work hours of an able-bodied adult without dependents (ABAWD) fall below an average of 20 hours a week

Reported changes that affect the cash budget also affect the SNAP budget

Income exceeds 130% of the Federal Poverty Income Guideline (FPIG) limits

NOTE: Any other changes are not required to be reported until SAR review.

Explain what information the household must verify and how they can verify it.

Give the household a toll-free telephone number it may call to ask questions or to get help completing the form. This may be a local number that accepts collect calls or an 800 number.

Give special assistance to a household asking for help completing and returning the SAR form when the household's adult members have mental or physical disabilities, are non-English speaking, or lack reading or writing skills.

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(5)(ii)(D)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.2 The Semiannual Reporting System 2
heading: 571.21 County Assistance Office Responsibilities

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.2 The Semiannual Reporting System

body:
The CAO must enroll a household by the next payment date whose deadline can be met after an application is processed or when a household meets SAR requirements.

The system enrolls a household in SAR by using one of the following codes:

1 —Meets SAR requirements

6 —Exempt; meets no SAR requirements
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.2 The Semiannual Reporting System 3
heading: 571.22 Beginning Enrollment in the Semiannual Reporting System

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.2 The Semiannual Reporting System

body:
The CAO must determine the enrollment period based on the household's circumstances.

A household is required to report semiannually unless it meets an exception for SAR enrollment. See Section 571.11 .

When a household reports a change that requires SAR enrollment, the CAO must enroll the household in SAR at the time the household reports the change. The CAO must let the household know about the new SAR reporting requirements.

When a household reports a change that requires removal from SAR, the CAO must remove the household from SAR at the next deadline that can be met. . The CAO must advise the household of the new non-SAR reporting requirements.

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing September 30, 2008
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.2 The Semiannual Reporting System 4
heading: 571.23 Length of Enrollment

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.2 The Semiannual Reporting System

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.3 Reviewing the Semiannual Reporting Form
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.3 Reviewing the Semiannual Reporting Form

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.3 Reviewing the Semiannual Reporting Form

body:
The CAO will review each SAR form received for timeliness and completeness. The CAO will track each SAR form received by the SAR form due date as either complete or incomplete in the CFSAR system. Tracking can be completed until the last day to track the SAR form has passed.

If the household provides a complete SAR form or SAR L/IN, with the required verification, the CAO will adjust benefits for the next semiannual period and establish a new SAR period.

The CAO must review each SAR form to make sure the household reported the following changes for all members:

7 CFR § 273.12(a)

Who lives in the household

Earned income

Child or dependent care costs

Child support paid for children outside of the household

Unearned income

Resources (for non Categorically eligible households) in excess of $2,000, or $3,000 for a household with at least one member age 60 or older or permanently disabled

Address and shelter/utility costs

The household must verify changes in::

Unreported or new gross earned income

Gross unearned income, only if there was a change in the amount received

Child or dependent care payments, if the household reports a change and it is questionable

Child support payments, if the reported amount has changed

Medical expenses, if the household reports a change in these expenses

Address and shelter or utility costs, if they have changed and are questionable

Any other changes the household reports that may affect eligibility or benefit level

NOTE: The SNAP case will not be closed if the household fails to report and verify medical expenses at application, SAR, during the SAR period or renewal. If medical expenses are not reported and verified, the household will not receive the deduction.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.3 Reviewing the Semiannual Reporting Form 1
heading: 571.3 Reviewing the Semiannual Reporting Form

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.3 Reviewing the Semiannual Reporting Form

body: The SAR form is considered timely if the CAO receives the form on or before the extended due date.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.3 Reviewing the Semiannual Reporting Form 2
heading: 571.31 Timely Semiannual Reporting Form

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.3 Reviewing the Semiannual Reporting Form

body:
When the household submits a complete SAR form by the last day to track it, benefits are issued on the regular SNAP issuance date . The SAR form is complete if all of the following requirements are met:

All questions except for #5 (only the shelter/utility part), #6, and #7 are answered completely.

NOTE: Failure to answer #5 (only the shelter/utility part), #6, or #7 or to give verification does not make the SAR form incomplete. For the household to receive these deductions, however, the questions must be answered, with verification. If the conditions are not met, the CAO must determine SNAP eligibility and benefit amount without considering the shelter or utility, child care, or child support deduction.

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(4)(i)

The form is signed by the payment name , an authorized representative (AR) , or a responsible household member.

Acceptable verification is attached for the following:

Gross earned income

7 CFR § 273.12(c)

A questionable reported change in child care expenses or expenses for the care of a sick or disabled person that results in a deduction affecting the benefit amount

A change in gross unearned income

A change in the amount of support paid to someone outside of the household

Any change in residence and the resulting change in shelter/utility costs

NOTE: Verification of questionable shelter/utility costs is necessary. The CAO may need to contact a third party, such as the landlord, to request verification.

Resources which exceed 2,000 or 3,250 for non Categorically Eligible households.

Tracking of question #8 should be completed as follows:

For households that are CE, track question #8 as complete

For households that are not CE, track question #8 as incomplete if the resource or verification is not provided.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.3 Reviewing the Semiannual Reporting Form 3
heading: 571.32 Complete Semiannual Reporting Form

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.3 Reviewing the Semiannual Reporting Form

body:
Benefits are suspended when a household fails to submit a completed SAR form. Good cause must be established within 30 days of the extended due date. The household may claim good cause verbally or in writing, on the SAR form, on the L/IN, or by letter.

NOTE: If benefits have been suspended or terminated for failure to submit a SAR form, the CAO will reinstate or reopen benefits when good cause is established, without the need for an application.

The CAO will evaluate the claim of good cause. Good cause includes, but is not limited to:

Circumstances beyond the control of the client that are not likely to recur, such as serious illness, death, accident, or mail delays caused by the postal service

NOTE: The CAO must review the validity of good-cause claims each time they occur during the six-month period.

Illiteracy, language problems, mental handicap, or any similar continuous circumstances

7 CFR § 273.21(c)(7)

NOTE: The CAO must provide special help in completing and filing the SAR form to a household granted good cause for this reason. The CAO may refer the household to a community resource, relative, friend, neighbor, or an agency for help completing the form. The CAO must record the referral in the case record. The validity of the claim is not reviewed again unless the community resource becomes unavailable.

The CAO will determine if good cause exists. A supervisory level review of the good cause determination must be made. The supervisor will:

Review the good-cause determination.

Support or deny the claim of good cause.

Return the case to the worker to take proper action.

Sign the authorization/instruction sheet ( PW 764 ).

The case record will be returned to the worker to complete.

The decision will be recorded in the case record

If good cause does not exist, the CAO must send the household a Confirming Notice to close the case. The notice must state that good cause was not established.

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing September 30, 2008
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.3 Reviewing the Semiannual Reporting Form 4
heading: 571.33 Good Cause

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.3 Reviewing the Semiannual Reporting Form

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.4 Processing Semiannual Reporting Forms
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.4 Processing Semiannual Reporting Forms

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.4 Processing Semiannual Reporting Forms

body:
If the CAO tracks the SAR form as complete, benefits will continue.

If the CAO tracks the SAR form as incomplete or does not track it at all, the system generates a SAR L/IN and suspends the case.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.4 Processing Semiannual Reporting Forms 1
heading: 571.4 Processing Semiannual Reporting Forms

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.4 Processing Semiannual Reporting Forms

body:
The CAO will review the SAR form. If it is complete and received by the due date, the CAO will:

7 CFR § 273.21(j)(1)

Track the SAR form as complete by the last day to track it.

Determine continued eligibility and benefit amount using the information on the SAR form.

Contact the household and record the contact results in the case record if the SAR form is complete but it shows a change that will affect benefits and verification of the change is not attached.

Contact the household and record the contact results in the case record if the SAR form is complete but the information is incorrect or inconsistent with information provided or known by the CAO.

Mail a Confirming Notice to tell the household of any action taken, such as suspension , termination, or change in the benefit. The notice must include the reason for the action and be based on information reported on the SAR form. The household must receive the Confirming Notice no later than the next regular issuance date .

Return all original verification materials to the household.

Reminder: If the household also receives Medical Assistance, recompute eligibility using the reported changes.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.4 Processing Semiannual Reporting Forms 2
heading: 571.41 Complete SAR Form Received by Due Date

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.4 Processing Semiannual Reporting Forms

body:
When the CAO receives a SAR form after the SAR form due date on which all questions are answered completely and required verification is attached, the CAO will:

7 CFR § 273.21(j)

Track the SAR form as complete if it is received by the last day to track it and time permits tracking.

Determine continued eligibility and benefit amount using the information shown on the SAR form, and establish a new SAR period.

Reactivate, suspend, or terminate benefits.

Promptly mail a confirming notice to tell the household of any action taken, such as suspension, reinstatement , termination, or change. The notice must include the reason for the action and be based on information reported on the SAR form.

Return all original verification materials to the household.

Reminder: If the household also receives Medical Assistance, recompute eligibility using the reported changes.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.4 Processing Semiannual Reporting Forms 3
heading: 571.42 Complete SAR Form Received after Due Date

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.4 Processing Semiannual Reporting Forms

body:
When a SAR form is received before the due date but not all questions are answered completely or verification is not attached, the CAO will:

7 CFR § 273.21(j)(3)

Track the SAR form as incomplete before the last day to track it. (Do not hold it until missing information is received.)

Contact the household to clarify information. The system will automatically mail the L/IN.

If the household submits a completed SAR form or L/IN before the extended due date with required verification, determine continued eligibility and the benefit amount using the information on the form, and determine a new SAR period.

If the information is not received by the extended due date, determine good cause and record the decision in the case record. Reinstate the case if good cause is determined.

Mail a Confirming Notice to tell the household of any action taken, such as suspension, termination, or change. The notice must include the reason for the action and be based on information reported on the SAR form.

Return all original verification materials to the household.

Reminder: If the household also receives Medical Assistance, recompute eligibility using the reported changes.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.4 Processing Semiannual Reporting Forms 4
heading: 571.43 Incomplete SAR Form Received by Due Date

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.4 Processing Semiannual Reporting Forms

body:
When a SAR form is received after the SAR form due date but by the last day to track the SAR form and not all questions are answered or no verification is attached, the CAO will:

7 CFR § 273.21(j)(3)

Track the SAR form as incomplete, if time permits.

NOTE: The CFSAR system suspends benefits when the CAO either tracks the SAR form as incomplete or does not track the SAR form because the household did not turn in a completed form in time for the CAO to track it. The system will automatically send an L/IN to the household for completion.

Determine continued eligibility and benefit amount using the information provided if the household turns in either a complete SAR form or a complete L/IN by the extended due date, and establish a new SAR period.

7 CFR § 273.12(c)

Promptly mail a confirming notice to tell the household of any action taken, such as suspension, termination, or change. The notice must include the reason for the action and be based on information reported on the SAR form.

Reinstate benefits, if applicable.

Return all original verification materials to the household.

Reminder: If the household also receives Medical Assistance, recompute eligibility using the reported changes

Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing September 30, 2008
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.4 Processing Semiannual Reporting Forms 5
heading: 571.44 Incomplete SAR Form Received after Due Date

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.4 Processing Semiannual Reporting Forms

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.5 Reviewing the Late/Incomplete Notice
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.5 Reviewing the Late/Incomplete Notice

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.5 Reviewing the Late/Incomplete Notice

body:
When the household submits a complete Late/Incomplete Notice (L/IN) by the extended due date, benefits are reinstated and provided no later than 10 days after the normal issuance date . (See Chapter 571, Appendix B .)

7 CFR § 273.21 (j)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.5 Reviewing the Late/Incomplete Notice 1
heading: 571.5 Reviewing the Late/Incomplete Notice

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.5 Reviewing the Late/Incomplete Notice

body: The L/IN is considered timely if the form is received on or before the extended due date shown on the notice.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.5 Reviewing the Late/Incomplete Notice 2
heading: 571.51 Timely Late/Incomplete Notice

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.5 Reviewing the Late/Incomplete Notice

body:
An L/IN is complete if it meets all of the following requirements:

All questions except for #5 (only the shelter/utility part), #6, or #7 are answered completely.

NOTE: Failure to answer #5 (only the shelter/utility part), #6, or #7 or to give verification for those answers does not make the L/IN form incomplete. However, for a household to receive these deductions, the questions must be answered, with verification. If the conditions are not met, the CAO must determine SNAP eligibility or the benefit amount without considering the shelter or utility, child care, or child support deduction.

7 CFR § 273.12 (a)(6)

The form is signed by the payee , an authorized representative (AR) , or a responsible household member.

Acceptable verification is attached for:

Gross earned income

A questionable reported change in child care expenses or expenses for the care of a sick or disabled person that results in a deduction affecting the benefit amount

A change in gross unearned income

A change in the amount of support paid to someone outside of the household

A change in residence and the resulting change in shelter/utility costs (Verification of questionable shelter/utility costs is necessary. The CAO may need to contact a third party, such as the landlord, for verification.)

a change in medical expenses

Resources that exceed $2,000 or $3,250 for non Categorically Eligible (CE) households.

Tracking of question #8 should be completed as follows:

For households that are CE, track question #8 as complete

For households that are not CE, track question #8 as incomplete if the resource or verification is not provided.

Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing September 30, 2008
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.5 Reviewing the Late/Incomplete Notice 3
heading: 571.52 Complete L/IN

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.5 Reviewing the Late/Incomplete Notice

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice

body: The CAO will determine continued eligibility and benefit amount based on the information shown on the Late/Incomplete Notice (L/IN) .
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice 1
heading: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice

body:
When an L/IN is received by the extended due date and is complete with all verification attached, the CAO will:

7 CFR § 273.21(k)(2)

Not track the L/IN.

Determine continued eligibility and benefit amount based on the information shown on the L/IN, and establish a new SAR period.

Promptly mail a Confirming Notice to let the household know about any action taken, such as reinstatement , termination, or change. The notice must include the reason for the action and be based on information reported on the L/IN.

Reinstate benefits by the CAO deadline for reactivating suspended cases. (This ensures that the case remains active in the CFSAR system.)

Return all original verification to the household.

Reminder: If the household also receives Medical Assistance, recompute eligibility using the reported changes.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice 2
heading: 571.61 Timely and Complete L/IN

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice

body:
When the CAO receives a L/IN before the extended due date but not all questions are answered completely or verification is not attached, the CAO will:

Not track the L/IN.

Record in the case record that benefits are terminated because the household failed to complete the L/IN.

The L/IN serves as a confirming notice, an additional confirming notice is not required to be sent.

If the missing information is provided, check for good cause:

record the good-cause decision in the case record, and take the proper action based on good cause. Reinstate the case if benefits were suspended.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice 3
heading: 571.62 Timely and Incomplete L/IN

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice

body:
The CAO will reinstate benefits if a complete SAR form or L/IN is received after the last day for system override but by the extended due date.

NOTE: The CAO will send a Confirming Notice to let the household know about the actions to be taken.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice 4
heading: 571.63 L/IN Received by the Extended Due Date

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice

body:
When an L/IN is not received by the extended due date, the CAO will:

Not track the L/IN.

Send no further notice of termination. (The L/IN serves as notice of closing.)

Record in the case record that benefits are terminated because the household failed to complete an L/IN on time.

Determine good cause:

Record the good-cause decision in the case record, and take the proper action based on good cause. Reinstate the case if benefits are suspended.

Review the weekly activity report for cases suspended by CFSAR because the household did not turn in the L/IN on time. Close the case if it is appropriate.

Reminder: If the household also receives Medical Assistance, recompute eligibility using the reported changes.

Updated March 21, 2024, replacing March 1, 2012
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice 5
heading: 571.64 L/IN Not Received by the Extended Due Date

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.6 Processing the Late/Incomplete Notice

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.7 Failure to Turn in a SAR Form or a Complete L/IN by the Extended Due Date
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.7 Failure to Turn in a SAR Form or a Complete L/IN by the Extended Due Date

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.7 Failure to Turn in a SAR Form or a Complete L/IN by the Extended Due Date

body:
The CFSAR system will suspend benefits for any household that does not submit either a SAR form or complete L/IN by the extended due date, no notice is required.

If there is no response to the L/IN, the CAO will record in the case record that the case is suspended.

Do not send notification of termination of benefits as the L/IN serves as the notice of closing.

If the case is reinstated due to good cause , the CAO will record the good-cause reason in the case record.

If the case is later reinstated after the CFSAR update deadline, the CAO will enter the correct CFSAR code to reenroll the case in the CFSAR system.

7 CFR 273.12(d)

Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing September 30, 2008
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.7 Failure to Turn in a SAR Form or a Complete L/IN by the Extended Due Date 1
heading: 571.7 Failure to Submit a Semiannual Reporting Form or a Complete Late/Incomplete Notice by the Extended Due Date

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.7 Failure to Turn in a SAR Form or a Complete L/IN by the Extended Due Date

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.8 One Month of Zero Issuance and Termination of Benefits
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.8 One Month of Zero Issuance and Termination of Benefits

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.8 One Month of Zero Issuance and Termination of Benefits

body:
The CAO will not suspend benefits if the information shown on the SAR form or the L/IN shows ineligibility that is expected to last for one month only.

7 CFR § 273.21(n)

Example: A household submits a SAR form in July saying the household will receive a one-time payment from a relative in August. The payment, combined with other income, makes the household ineligible for one month. The CAO will not suspend benefits. The household is notified of the zero issuance for August and the continuation of benefits beginning in September. The CAO must remove the income for September.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.8 One Month of Zero Issuance and Termination of Benefits 1
heading: 571.8 One Month of Zero Issuance and Termination of Benefits

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.8 One Month of Zero Issuance and Termination of Benefits

body:
When a household submits a SAR form showing it received income that is not expected to last, the CAO will average only the income that is expected to continue during the semiannual period.

Example: A household submits a SAR form in January and provides all pay stubs for December. The person got overtime pay in the holiday season. The overtime is not expected to continue. The CAO will not average the overtime pay when determining the income to adjust for the next SAR period.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.8 One Month of Zero Issuance and Termination of Benefits 2
heading: 571.81 Processing one Month of Zero Issuance or a Temporary Increase in Income

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.8 One Month of Zero Issuance and Termination of Benefits

body:
The CAO will terminate a household's benefits if the information shown on the SAR form or L/IN shows ineligibility that is expected to last for more than one month. The CAO will send a Confirming Notice to the household.

7 CFR § 273.21(m)

The household must submit a new application if benefits are terminated because of eligibility criteria such as excess income and/or resources.

The CAO will not require a new application if the household reapplies within 30 days of the closing and/or complies with the requirement (including SAR) that originally closed the case. SNAP benefits will be prorated to the date the household provides the information and verification.

The renewal date will remain the same as previously established in e CIS . However, the household had the option to submit a new application. If the CAO receives a new application, the CAO will review it for expedited services.

The SAR form is not required for processing expedited services or ongoing benefits.

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing September 30, 2008
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.8 One Month of Zero Issuance and Termination of Benefits 3
heading: 571.82 Termination of Benefits

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.8 One Month of Zero Issuance and Termination of Benefits

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.9 Appeal and Fair Hearing Process
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.9 Appeal and Fair Hearing Process

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.9 Appeal and Fair Hearing Process

body:
Households required to participate in SAR may appeal any adverse action within 90 days from the action or loss of benefits. (See Supplemental Handbook, Chapter 870 .)

7 CFR § 273.15(a)

Benefits will continue until the end of the certification period or until the hearing result if the appeal challenges an action based on noncompliance with SAR requirements and the household claims it submitted a SAR form or a L/IN .

7 CFR § 273.15(k)

NOTE: The household must complete a SAR form for the reporting period in question.

The CAO will not continue benefits if the appeal is based on the requirement to participate in SAR, and the household does not complete the SAR form.

The CAO will change the benefit amount using information provided on the SAR form, except for factors that are the reason for the appeal.

Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing September 30, 2008
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.9 Appeal and Fair Hearing Process 1
heading: 571.9 Appeal and Fair Hearing Process

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.9 Appeal and Fair Hearing Process

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.10 Renewal of Semiannual Reporting Cases
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.10 Renewal of Semiannual Reporting Cases

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 571.10 Renewal of Semiannual Reporting Cases

body:
A household required to report semiannually must be assigned a certification period effective the first month of certification, including any expedited issuance. (See Chapter 575 for determining the length of the certification period . )

The CAO must renew the household before the SAR form due date in the 12th month. The renewal interview must include the requirements in Chapter 576 .

To coordinate the SAR process with the renewal process, the CAO will:

Send the PA 10-SP form to the household so that it arrives before the end of the month before the last month of the certification period.

Attempt to schedule the renewal interview before the SAR form due date in the last month of the certification period.

Enter the renewal before the regular SNAP issuance deadline to continue benefits.

7 CFR § 273.21(q)

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing September 30, 2008

heading: 571.10 Renewal of Semiannual Reporting Cases

body:
Click here for the Semiannual Reporting Form ( PA 564 ).

Click here for the Late/Incomplete Notice ( PA 564-A ).

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing September 30, 2008
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Appendix A: The Semiannual Reporting Form and Late/Incomplete Notice 1
heading: Appendix A: The Semiannual Reporting Form and Late/Incomplete Notice

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Appendix A: The Semiannual Reporting Form and Late/Incomplete Notice

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Appendix B: Generation of the Late/Incomplete Notice
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Appendix B: Generation of the Late/Incomplete Notice

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Appendix B: Generation of the Late/Incomplete Notice

body:
When the SAR form is not returned or is returned late or incomplete, the Late/Incomplete Notice (L/IN, PA 564-A ) is sent. (See Chapter 571, Appendix A .) The L/IN serves as the Advance Notice to close for the cash and SNAP programs. The L/IN includes:

Questions from the SAR that were not answered, along with the pre-populated information as it appeared on the SAR form.

The extended due date when the L/IN must be in the CAO .

NOTE: The L/IN does not serve as an Advance Notice for the Medical Assistance program. To close or change a Medical Assistance program through the SAR process, the CAO must send an Advance Notice. See Medical Assistance Handbook, Section 377.4 .

Question #1 will always be printed and answered on the L/IN for use as a reference for the recipient to answer other questions. If this question was not answered or completed by the recipient, the question “Did anyone move into or out of your household ?” will also appear on the L/IN.

The completion of the tracking screen based on the information provided on the SAR form will directly impact the information on the L/IN. For example:

If all questions are incomplete, and the ALL boxes on the SAR form and on the CCMRTK screen are checked, then all questions and data from the SAR form will be printed on the L/IN.

If the SAR form is not signed, and the UNSIGNED boxes on the SAR form and on the CCMRTK screen are checked, then the L/IN will tell the client, “You did not sign your semiannual reporting form. Please sign the certification section and return the form.”

1. Did anyone move into or out of your household? If yes, list who and their relationship to you.

Question #1 asks for a report of changes in who lives in the household. Block 1 is checked if question #1 is not answered or is incomplete. There is no longer a requirement to track verification for this question. The caseworker should contact the recipient as needed.

2. Did any household member start a new job, change a job, or stop working? If yes, list any changes, such as job start date, end date, date of first pay, how often paid). Provide proof (pay stubs, employer statements, etc.).

Question #2 asks for a report of employment changes. Block 2 is checked if question #2 is not answered and both the question and the data are printed on the L/IN. If the household reports changes, verification must be provided. Block 2V is checked if proof of changes is inadequate or not attached, and the statement “You did not provide proof of any changes” is printed. If both Block 2 and 2V are checked, the question and the data will be printed on the L/IN.

3. Provide proof (pay stubs, employer statements, etc.) of all work income any household member received in the month of [reporting month]

Question #3 asks for a report of earned income. If a household member has earned income in the reporting month, proof must be provided. If all proof is not provided or is inadequate, Block 3V is checked, and the following statement is printed on the L/IN: “You did not provide proof of any changes."

4. Did any household member lose or start receiving income or have a change in amount? If yes, list any changes. Provide proof (award letter, support court orders, etc.).

Question #4 asks for a report of unearned income changes. Block 4 is checked if question #4 is not answered and both the question and the data are printed on the L/IN. If the recipient reports changes, verification must be provided. Block 4V is checked if proof of changes is inadequate or not attached, and the statement "You did not provide proof of any changes" is printed. If both Block 4 and 4V are checked, the question and the data will be printed on the L/IN.

5. Is the address on this form your current address? If no, what is your new address? Provide proof (Examples: lease, landlord statement, deed, etc.).

If you receive SNAP benefits and you have moved, what are your shelter (rent/mortgage) and utility costs? Do you pay for your own heating and/or air conditioning?

Question #5 asks for a report of an address change. Block 5 is checked if the question is not answered, and Question #5 is printed on the L/IN. Data is not filled in for this question, but the recipient has space to provide a new address. The portion of the question regarding shelter and utility costs does not need to be answered or verified and will not be tracked. Recipients are advised in the Instructions section that if this information is not provided, it may result in less or no SNAP benefits.

6. Did any household member have a change in the amount he is requested to pay? If yes, list any changes. Provide copy of support court order or letter and proof of payment.

Question #6 asks for a report of the amount of support paid for children outside the household. This question is not required to be tracked and has no corresponding block on the SAR form. Recipients are advised in the instructions that changes in support paid will affect SNAP benefits and that they may receive less or no SNAP benefits if this information is not provided and verified. This question will appear on the L/IN only if the SAR form is not received or if the ALL box is checked on the SAR form.

7. Are there any changes? If yes, list any changes. Provide copy of bill or statement from caregiver.

Question #7 asks for a report of the amount paid for child care or for care of a sick or disabled person. Recipients are advised in the instructions that changes in support paid will affect SNAP benefits and that they may receive less or no SNAP benefits if this information is not provided and verified. This question will appear on the L/IN only if the SAR form is not received or if the ALL box is checked on the SAR form.

8. Has the information in this section changed? Does any household member have resources not listed above? If you answered yes to either question, list any changes. Provide proof (copy of bank statement, vehicle registration, etc.)

Question #8 asks for a report of household resources. Block 8 is checked if the recipient does not answer either or both questions.

Exception: The CAO must not mark this question as incomplete, regardless of the answers, if resource information is not required in the eligibility determination according to Medical Assistance Handbook, Section 340.1 .

Question #8 and the data information are printed on the L/IN. If required changes are reported, proof of resource changes must be provided. Block 8V is checked if the proof of changes is inadequate or not attached, and the statement "You did not provide proof of any changes" is printed. If both Block 8 and 8V are checked, the question and the data will be printed on the L/IN.

Reminder: The CAO worker must always narrate any incomplete verification. This will make it easier for CAO and Change Center staff to help recipients with any questions on what information must be provided.

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing September 30, 2008

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 572: Mass Changes
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 572: Mass Changes

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 572: Mass Changes

body:
Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Handbook

Chapter 572: Mass Changes
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 572: Mass Changes 1
heading: 572 Title

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 572: Mass Changes

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.1 General Policy
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.1 General Policy

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.1 General Policy

body:
State and federal governments periodically make changes that affect an entire caseload or part of a caseload. These are known as mass changes . They affect the following:

SNAP changes

Public Assistance ( TANF / GA ) grant changes

Federal benefit changes

7 CFR § 273.12(e)

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing March 26, 2004
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.1 General Policy 1
heading: 572.1 General Policy

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.1 General Policy

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.2 SNAP Changes
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.2 SNAP Changes

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.2 SNAP Changes

body:
SNAP changes include adjustments to the following:

7 CFR § 273.12(e)(1)

Income eligibility standards (gross/net income test and eligibility limit)

Shelter and dependent care deductions

Thrifty Food Plan (maximum benefit )

Standard deduction

Utility standard

Homeless shelter deduction

Resource limits

The CAO does not need to notify the household of mass changes in SNAP. The system will issue a notice to the household.

7 CFR § 273.12(e)(1)(ii)

Updated September 30, 2024, replacing March 1, 2012
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.2 SNAP Changes 1
heading: 572.2 SNAP Changes

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.2 SNAP Changes

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.3 Public Assistance Grant Changes
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.3 Public Assistance Grant Changes

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.3 Public Assistance Grant Changes

body:
Public assistance grant changes include changes to the following:

7 CFR § 273.12(e)(2)

Eligibility criteria

Benefit level

When an overall adjustment is made to TANF / GA grants, a household receiving TANF/GA and SNAP benefits must have the new grant amount applied to its SNAP budget.

If the CAO is notified of the TANF/GA grant change at least 30 days in advance, SNAP benefits must be changed in the same month the grant change takes effect. When the CAO is not given sufficient notice, the change must be made no later than the month following the month of the TANF/GA grant change.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.3 Public Assistance Grant Changes 1
heading: 572.3 Public Assistance Grant Changes

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.3 Public Assistance Grant Changes

body:
For most budgets, the Client Information System (CIS) will apply the new grant amount, compute the benefit amount, let the household know about the change, and send a notice to the case worker for control.

7 CFR § 273.12(e)(2)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.3 Public Assistance Grant Changes 2
heading: 572.31 Client Information System Processing

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.3 Public Assistance Grant Changes

body:
The CAO must manually compute cases that cannot be processed by the system. The CAO must:

Recalculate the benefit amount using the new grant amount; and

Notify the household of the change, using a Confirming Notice ( PA/FS 162-C ).

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing April 16, 2008

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.4 Federal Benefit Changes
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.4 Federal Benefit Changes

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.4 Federal Benefit Changes

body:
Federal changes include periodic cost-of-living adjustments (COLAs) and other mass changes to the following:

7 CFR § 273.12(e)(3)

Social Security Retirement, Survivor's, and Disability Insurance (RSDI)

Supplemental Security Income (SSI)

Veteran's Administration (VA) benefits

Black Lung benefits

Railroad Retirement benefits

Other federal benefits
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.4 Federal Benefit Changes 1
heading: 572.4 Federal Benefit Changes

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.4 Federal Benefit Changes

body:
When there is a COLA or other mass change in federal benefits:

SAR households do not have to report mass grant changes unless the mass grant change causes the total gross household income to go above the 130% FPIG limit.

Non-SAR households with unearned income have to report if the mass grant change causes the household’s gross monthly unearned income to change by more than $50.

When information about a mass change is not available through the I ncome Eligibility Verification System (IEVS), such as for VA, Black Lung, and Railroad Retirement benefits, the CAO must contact the household to determine whether there is an income change.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.4 Federal Benefit Changes 2
heading: 572.41 Reporting Requirements

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.4 Federal Benefit Changes

body:
The CAO must adjust the benefit amount when it learns about a mass change.

The CAO must change the benefit amount by the time the second benefit is issued after the month in which the change occurred.

If the household's certification period ends before issuance of the second benefit, the adjustment must be made at renewal .
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.4 Federal Benefit Changes 3
heading: 572.42 Timing

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.4 Federal Benefit Changes

body:
The CAO must verify mass changes in RSDI benefits through BENDEX , State Data Exchange (SDX), or IEVS.

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(7)

VA, Railroad Retirement, and Black Lung information is not available through system matches. Households receiving these benefits may or may not receive the COLA. The information must be verified case by case.

A household not required to be enrolled in SAR does not need to provide verification until the next renewal if there is an unearned-income change of $50 or less.

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(8)

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing April 16, 2008
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.4 Federal Benefit Changes 4
heading: 572.43 Verification

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.4 Federal Benefit Changes

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.5 Notice
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.5 Notice

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.5 Notice

body:
The CAO must send a Confirming Notice ( PA/FS 162-C ) to notify the household of a mass change in the cash grant or federal benefits.

The CAO must take the following actions:

Send a PA/FS 162-C to SNAP-only households when increasing, decreasing, or closing benefits during the certification period .

Send a PA/FS 162-C to combination cash assistance/SNAP or Medical Assistance (MA)/SNAP households when a PA/FS 162-A is not required to decrease or close cash assistance or MA benefits.

Send the PA/FS 162-C so that the household receives it no later than the day the household receives the benefit. This applies to all households, regardless of how they are to report changes.

If the change in benefits is made at renewal , send a Notice to Applicant ( PA/FS 162 ). The household must receive the notice no later than the end of the current certification period.

NOTE: If an Advance Notice ( PA/FS 162-A ) is sent to let a household know about an adverse action to cash assistance or MA benefits (or both), the same notice can be used for a change in the benefit amount. The CAO must send the PA/FS 162-A so that the household will receive the notice no later than the day the benefit is received.

7 CFR § 273.12(e)(4)

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing April 16, 2008

heading: 572.5 Notice

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.5 Notice

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.6 Fair Hearing
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.6 Fair Hearing

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 572.6 Fair Hearing

body:
A household has the right to request a fair hearing when benefits are adjusted because of a mass change . If the appeal is filed timely, the CAO must not take action to reduce or terminate benefits until the hearing decision is received.

7 CFR § 273.12(e)(5)

Benefits continue at their previous level if:

7 CFR § 273.12(e)(6)

A fair hearing is requested timely;

The right to continued benefits is not waived; and

The fair hearing is based on an improper computation of benefits or improper application or interpretation of federal laws or regulations.

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing March 26, 2004
