citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 575: Certification Periods
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 575: Certification Periods

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 575: Certification Periods

body:
Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Handbook

Chapter 575: Certification Periods
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 575: Certification Periods 1
heading: 575 Title

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 575: Certification Periods

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.1 General Policy
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.1 General Policy

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.1 General Policy

body:
Elderly/Disabled Simplified Application Process (ESAP) Certification Period , PFS-22450-575 (Published March 13, 2026)

The CAO must assign a certification period once it determines that a household is eligible to participate in SNAP. A certification period is the period of time within which a household is eligible to receive benefits. The CAO assigns the longest certification period possible based on the household's circumstances and characteristics. See Section 575.2 .

NOTE: The expedited and ongoing certification periods may be authorized separately and considered separate certification periods. SNAPH 575.1 advises that the CAO must assign a certification period once it determines that a household is eligible to participate in SNAP. Following the expedited OTI, the ongoing SNAP determination was pended for verification. Once the verification is received and the eligibility determined, the certification period is assigned based on the beginning date of the ongoing benefit.

Example: A household applies on 5/17/24 and is determined to be eligible for expedited benefits, but also reports income changes which must be verified for ongoing benefits. The expedited benefits are issued for 5/17/24-6/30/24 in a 2-month certification period and the case is pended for income verification for the ongoing benefit. When the verification is received, the case is processed with a begin date of 7/1/24 for the ongoing benefit, so the certification period for the ongoing benefit will expire on 6/30/25 if the household qualifies for the standard 12-month certification period, 6/30/26 if they qualify for a 24-month certification period or 6/30/27 for FS75 cases.

7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)(iii)

SNAP benefits stop at the end of each certification period. The CAO continues benefits only after renewal of eligibility. See Chapter 576, Renewal .

7 CFR § 273.10(f)

The household must receive written notice of the begin and end dates of the certification period. See Section 575.4.

The certification period is based on calendar months. It begins the first day of the first month of eligibility.

Examples:

A household applies in January and is eligible for benefits in January. The application is processed in February. The certification period begins in January.

A household applies in January and is ineligible for benefits in January. The CAO determines the household’s SNAP eligibility begins in February and the certification period begins in February.

An eligible household that is not receiving a benefit because of proration is considered eligible in that month. The month that the benefits are prorated to $0 is the first month of the certification period.

See Appendix A for Certification/Renewal Chart.

Updated April 10, 2026, replacing June 10, 2015
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.1 General Policy 1
heading: 575.1 General Policy

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.1 General Policy

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods

body:
The CAO must assign the longest certification period possible, using the guidelines below.

7 CFR § 273.10(f)

NOTE: The CAO must not base the certification period on the household 's enrollment in SAR . For SAR policy relating to certification periods, see Chapter 571.11 .

575.21 Households Assigned a Thirty-Six Month Certification Period

The CAO may assign a 36 month certification period to the household if:

All members are elderly or disabled and

Have countable unearned income only

NOTE: These budgets are signified by budget and program status code of FS 75

NOTE: If these households have zero income because they are part of the Elderly/Disabled Simplified Application Process(ESAP) they should receive a 36 month certification period.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods 1
heading: 575.2 Guidelines for Assigning Certification Periods

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods

body:
The CAO may assign 24-month certification period to the household if :

All adult members are elderly or disabled with countable income . For the definition of elderly/disabled, see Chapter 510.21 Definitions of Elderly and Disabled individuals.

NOTE: If all adults in the household are elderly or disabled, they may receive the 24-month certification period even if they have earned income; however, they must be assigned a 6-month certification period if they have zero income as indicated below.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods 2
heading: 575.22 Households Assigned a Twenty-Four Month Certification Period

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods

body:
The CAO may assign a 12-month certification period if the household has:

Earned income, or

Unearned income.

All adult members are not elderly or disabled

If the household no longer receives earned or unearned income, the certification period does not change.

The CAO may also assign a 12-month certification period to a Pennsylvanians with Employment and/or Engagement Requirements (PEER) household that is not subject to time limits if the household lives in a geographically waived area. See Chapter 575.25 .

NOTE: Households whose income ends in the initial month and zero income is being counted for ongoing benefits are considered a zero-income household as indicated in Section 575.24 and should receive the appropriate certification period of 6-months or less depending on PEER status.

7 CFR § 273.10(f)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods 3
heading: 575.23 Households Assigned a Twelve-Month Certification Period

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods

body:
The CAO must assign a six-month certification period to the household if it is:

A migrant or seasonal farm worker household;

A zero-income household unless an ESAP (FS75) household; or

Has only earned or unearned income that is exempt.

NOTE: The CAO must consider a household whose only income is from cash assistance as a household with exempt unearned income.

NOTE: Households which contain one or more PEER, must be assigned the certification period as indicated in Section 575.25.

7 CFR § 273.10(f)(3)(ii)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods 4
heading: 575.24 Households Assigned a Certification Period of Six Months

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods

body:
If the PEER is not currently meeting the work requirement and is not exempt by law, the CAO must base the length of the certification period on the number of months the PEER is allowed to receive SNAP benefits. See Chapter 536.3 .

Updated April 10, 2026, replacing April 18, 2025
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods 5
heading: 7 CFR § 273.11(a)

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.2 Guidelines for Certification Periods

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households

body:
When a change in circumstances requires cash assistance to stop and the CAO does not have enough information to determine the SNAP benefit amount, the CAO must take the following actions:

Send a request for clarification or proof.

If the household fails to respond to the request, send the household an Advance Notice of Adverse Action advising the household of its plans to terminate the SNAP benefits.

The CAO must continue SNAP benefits during the appeal process if the household asks for a hearing on the proposed plan to stop cash assistance benefits.

7 CFR § 273.12(f)(3)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households 1
heading: 575.3 Shortening the Certification Periods for SNAP Households

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households

body:
The CAO must align all budgets that can be aligned at the next application, SAR review, renewal or reported change that results in an eligibility determination. See Appendix C for general guidelines to follow when aligning budgets within a case record.

NOTE: The renewal year for a 36-month ESAP budget will never align with the renewal year of a 12-month MA or TANF budget, however the CAO should still align the renewal month so the client recipient receives their notices for all budgets at the same time (the ESAP interim contact letter PA1937 and the MA/TANF renewal packets). If the recipient fails to complete their next MA/TANF renewal, those budgets can close and the recipient will still have the remaining two years of their three-year ESAP certification, thus getting their full certification period.

When a SNAP household enrolled in SAR applies for Cash or Medical Assistance (MA) using an application form ( PA 600 or PA 600R ) that includes SNAP, the CAO must align the renewal dates.

Exception: Alignment policy does not apply to Extended SNAP (FS 43) households.

NOTE: Remember that MA may be opened with a renewal period of less than 12 months. SNAP certification periods may not always have to be shortened to align renewal periods for all budgets.

The CAO may shorten the SNAP certification period by completing a renewal interview. If the household does not cooperate in providing the necessary information to complete the renewal, the CAO must close the budget.

NOTE: If the SNAP packet was mailed and not returned, the CAO will close SNAP using reason code 042

After the renewal, the CAO must:

Assign a new certification period that aligns with the Cash or MA renewal period.

Send a confirming notice to the household stating the benefit amount (even if it has not changed) and new certification period.

7 CFR § 273.29

The CAO is not required to issue supplemental benefits or submit an overpayment referral after completing the renewal if:

The individual does not submit verification of required information within 30 days but does provide it within 60 days

The CAO does not process the Cash or MA application timely.

If a household files an appeal within 15 days of the date the Confirming Notice is mailed because the CAO proposes to decrease SNAP benefits and elects to continue to receive the higher benefit amount, the CAO must continue the original benefit amount until the hearing decision or the certification period ends, whichever is earlier. The budgets cannot be aligned.

If the household appeals after 15 days but before 90 days, benefits continue in the reduced amount until the hearing decision. Budgets can be aligned.

The CAO must narrate all eligibility decisions and case actions made at application, SAR, renewal, reported change and verbal or written contact with the household. For renewals, the CAP must narrate how the renewal was conducted (face-to-face, mail, telephone). Case comments must also include the statement that data exchanges (DX) were reviewed for all individuals who may affect the households eligibility.

Examples:

1. A SNAP-only SAR household has a September 2025 through August 2026 certification period. The household's circumstances change and the household applies for and is determined eligible for MA in December 2025 . The CAO completes the SNAP renewal at the MA interview. Since the SNAP-only SAR household is now eligible for MA, the SNAP certification period is shortened and now runs December 2025 through November 2026 . The SNAP and MA SAR review date is May 2026 . The household's SNAP benefits are reduced as a result of the information provided on the application for Medicaid. The CAO sends the household a Confirming Notice using Option 6 informing it of the new benefit amount and new certification period.

2. A SNAP-only SAR household has a July 2025 through June 2026 certification period. The household's circumstances change. The household applies for Cash Assistance and is determined ineligible. The SNAP certification period does not change.

3. A SNAP / Medicaid SAR household has a July 2025 through June 2026 certification period. The households circumstances change. The household applies for and is authorized Cash Assistance effective February 2026 with a renewal date of February 2027 . The CAO completes a SNAP renewal at the Cash Assistance interview. The household's new SNAP certification period is March 2026 through February 2027 .

4. A SNAP/MA household has a certification period of August 2025 through July 2026 . The household applies for Cash Assistance on February 14, 2026 . The CAO denies the application because the household does not provide required verification within 30 days. On March 17th, the household provides all verification. The CAO authorizes Cash Assistance, setting a renewal date of March 16, 2027 . The CAO completes a SNAP renewal new SNAP certification period is April 2026 through March 2027 . The SNAP and Cash Assistance SAR due date is October 2026 . The CAO does not issue supplemental SNAP benefits or compete an overpayment referral for March 2026 .
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households 2
heading: 575.31 Budget Alignment

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households

body:
When opening a Cash Assistance or MA budget, the CAO must:

Process the case as a Program Add and be sure to select the appropriate method of verification for each item,

Enter all mandatory (*) fields on any additional screens that appear,

Update any necessary Individual Non-financial, Resource or Income information until the Execute Eligibility screen is presented. Click "Run Eligibility" button, and proceed through the subsequent screens,

At the SNAP Budget Authorization Screen, amend the SAR Due Date and the Reapp Due Date fields,

At the Post Authorization/Client Notice screen, ensure or change the FS00 notice to Notice 001, option 6,

Continue processing through the case and complete all mandatory (*) fields. Submit the case,

If the existing SNAP budget does not change as a result of the addition of the Cash or Medical Assistance budgets, the system will not issue Notice 001. Issue a manual notice to inform the household of the revised certification period,

Ensure comments are added to the case narrate to reflect the changed dates.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households 3
heading: 575.32 System Process for Shortening the SNAP Certification Period

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.3 Changes in Length of Certification Periods for Cash Assistance/SNAP Households

body:
On the Case Summary Screen, select "Maintenance" from the "What would you like to do today?" drop down and click the "Next" button.

On the Left-Hand Navigation bar, click on "Execute Eligibility",

Click "Run Eligibility",

After eligibility has run, click the "Next" button,

The next screen will be the "Eligibility Results" screen. Ensure the benefits are correct and then click the "Next" button.

For a SAR date change:

On the "Budget Authorization" screen, click on the calendar or manually type in the SAR Due Date under SAR Due Date, then click on the "Next" button,

For a Reapplication Date change:

Click on the calendar or manually type in the Reapplication Due Date under Reapp due Date and then click on the "Next" button,

Continue through Case Processing, entering all relevant information to the case and completing all mandatory (*) fields through case commit.

7 CFR § 273.10(f)(4)

and FNS waiver #2030048, granted under the authority of 7 CFR 272.3(c)(1)(ii)

Updated April 10, 2026, replacing June 16, 2015

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.4 Certification Notices
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.4 Certification Notices

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 575.4 Certification Notices

body:
The CAO must provide the household with a Notice of Eligibility listing the begin and end dates of the certification period.

For an initial application, the CAO must provide the notice as soon as the eligibility determination is made, no later than 30 days from the application date.

7 CFR § 273.10(g)(1)

For a renewal , the CAO must provide the notice before the end of the current certification period .

7 CFR § 273.10(g)(2)

In expedited cases when the certification period is longer than one month and verification is pending, the notice must explain that benefits will stop if verification is not provided.

7 CFR § 273.10(g)(1)(i)(B)

The system will issue the PA 10-SP before the end of the certification period for any household with a certification period of three months or more.

7 CFR § 273.10(g)(2)

Updated April 10, 2026, replacing June 16, 2015

heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Appendix C: Guidelines for Budget Alignment

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Appendix C: Guidelines for Budget Alignment

body:
The following are general guidelines to follow when aligning budgets within case records:

IF AND THEN 1. There are multiple open budgets The budgets are being aligned Align to the program with the earliest SAR/renewal date 2. There is any open budget in the case An application is received for another benefit Align the new budget SAR/renewal to the existing open budget 3. There are multiple open budgets An application is received for another benefit First align the existing budgets following #1 above. Then align the new budget following #2 above. 4. There is an open MA and/or SNAP budget An application is received for Cash Assistance At the Cash Assistance application interview, gather information needed to complete renewal for all budgets.

Reviewed April 10, 2026, replacing June 16, 2015

heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 576: Renewal

body:
Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Handbook

Chapter 576: Renewal
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 576: Renewal 1
heading: 576 Title

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 576: Renewal

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.1 General Policy
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.1 General Policy

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.1 General Policy

body:
S upplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Closing SNAP Benefits before the end of the Certification Period when Renewal is not Complete, PFS-21170-576 (Published November 16, 2023)

TANF and SNAP Processing during the COVID-19 Emergency , PFS-19846-576 (Published April 15, 2020)

The CAO is responsible for completing, processing, and approving or denying applications for renewal .

7 CFR § 273.14(a)

The CAO must complete the renewal process before the end of a household 's current certification period .

The household must complete the renewal process timely to receive uninterrupted benefits.

If the time frame for submitting verification extends beyond the certification period, the CAO cannot complete a renewal until the verification is received.

The CAO must provide uninterrupted benefits within five working days if verification is received after the end of the certification period but within the 30-day time frame.

7 CFR § 273.14(b)(4)

If the renewal process is not completed before the end of the certification period, benefits will automatically stop. The CAO must send a Confirming Notice telling the household that benefits have stopped.

7 CFR § 273.14(b)

A household that is receiving Extended SNAP benefits is not renewed until the Extended SNAP period ends or the household believes it may receive more SNAP benefits because of changes in household circumstances.

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing May 3, 2007
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.1 General Policy 1
heading: 576.1 General Policy

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.1 General Policy

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.2 Notice of Expiration
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.2 Notice of Expiration

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.2 Notice of Expiration

body:
The CAO will receive a system -generated Notice of Expiration ( PA 10-SP ) for each household for which certification is about to end. The system will send the PA 10-SP to the CAO before the last month of the household's certification period .

7 CFR § 273.14(b)(1)

7 CFR § 273.14(b)(3)

The CAO must mail the PA 10-SP , a renewal form, and an Appointment Notice and Verification Checklist ( PA 253 ) to the household at least one day before the last month of the certification period.

The system will not generate a PA 10-SP for households certified for only one or two months.

The SNAP renewal for a combination cash assistance/SNAP household is completed at the same time the cash assistance renewal is completed. The PA 10-SP is not required if the joint cash assistance/SNAP renewal is completed within the certification period.

7 CFR § 273.14(b)(1)

For a household certified for only one or two months, the CAO must prepare the PA 10-SP and give it to the household, along with a renewal form, at the time of certification.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.2 Notice of Expiration 1
heading: 576.2 Notice of Expiration

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.2 Notice of Expiration

body:
The PA 10-SP generated by the system or initiated by the CAO must contain the following information:

7 CFR § 273.14(b)(1)

Case identification

Household name and address

CAO name, address, and phone number

Date the certification period ends

Date and time of the interview

The CAO needs to complete only the date and time of the interview for PA 10-SP s generated by the system.

For all PA 10-SP s, the original goes to the household, and the copy stays in the case record for three years.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.2 Notice of Expiration 2
heading: 576.21 Completion of the Notice of Expiration

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.2 Notice of Expiration

body:
The CAO must waive the required face-to-face office interview for SSI households at renewal. It is not necessary to assess the household hardship factors listed in Section 504.43 . The CAO must routinely complete telephone interview renewals for SSI households as follows:

1. Mail to the SSI household a PA 10-SP , a Telephone Interview Instructions for SSI Recipients Reapplying for SNAP ( PA 10-SP Addendum ), a renewal form, and an Appointment Notice and Verification Checklist ( PA 253 ), if needed, along with a return envelope.

2. Receive the completed, signed renewal form and any required verifications from the household before the scheduled telephone interview, as instructed on the PA 10-SP Addendum . The CAO may need to request additional verification items during the renewal telephone interview.

3. Telephone the SSI head of household or AR on the day and time shown on the PA 10-SP Addendum . Conduct the renewal telephone interview.

The CAO may choose to do a face-to-face office or home-visit interview for renewal for SSI households with questionable circumstances. The household may also request a face-to-face renewal interview to be completed at the CAO or the client's home. The SSI household may also choose to apply at a Social Security Administration office.

7 CFR § 273.14(b)(3)

The PA 10-SP Addendum copy, like the PA 10-SP copy, must be kept in the case record for three years.

Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing May 3, 2007
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.2 Notice of Expiration 3
heading: 576.22 Notice of Expiration and Addendum: Scheduling Telephone Interviews for SSI Households

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.2 Notice of Expiration

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.3 Timely Renewals
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.3 Timely Renewals

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.3 Timely Renewals

body:
The household completes the application process when it does all of the following:

7 CFR § 273.14(d)

Files a timely renewal form

Attends and completes an interview

Submits requested verification within 30 days from the request date

7 CFR § 273.14(e)

The CAO must process a timely application for renewal and provide uninterrupted benefits to any household found eligible. (See Section 576.31 .)

To be considered timely:

7 CFR § 273.14(c)

A semiannual review (SAR) household must submit a renewal form on or before the due date for the last month of certification.

7 CFR § 273.14(c)(1)

Example: A household's certification period ends June 6 in issuance cycle 7. The household must submit a renewal form before the SAR form due date of June 16.

A SAR household must submit a renewal form by the 15th day of the last month of certification.

Example: A household's certification period ends June 6 in issuance cycle 7. The household must submit a renewal form by June 15.

A SAR household certified for only one month must submit a renewal form within 15 days from the receipt of the PA 10-SP .

Example: A household certified for one month from March 7 to March 31 receives the PA 10-SP on March 5. It must submit a renewal form by March 20.

A non-SAR household certified for two months must submit a renewal form by the 15th day of the second month if the case was certified in the first month.

Example: A household is certified on April 8 for two months, April and May. It receives the PA 10-SP on April 8. The household must submit a renewal form by May 15.

If the case was certified in the second month, a non-SAR household certified for two months must submit a renewal form within 15 days from the date the PA 10-SP is received.

Example: A household is certified for two months, April and May, but the certification process is not completed until May 3. The household receives the PA 10-SP on May 3. It must submit a renewal form by May 18.

The CAO must allow the household 30 calendar days to submit required verification. The CAO must begin the 30-day count with the day after the initial request for verification.

7 CFR § 273.14(c)(3)

The CAO must provide uninterrupted benefits to households that file timely and provide verification within 30 days. (See Section 576.31 .)

The CAO must tell the household the verification requirements and record the request for verification in the case record.

Example: A household files a timely renewal form on March 14 and is interviewed on March 25. Additional verification is needed. The 30-day time frame is set to end April 24. The household provides the required verification on March 29. The household is entitled to uninterrupted benefits.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.3 Timely Renewals 1
heading: 576.3 Timely Renewals

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.3 Timely Renewals

body:
If a household (a) has been certified for one month or (b) has been certified for two months and is in the second month of the certification period, and if the household completes the renewal process timely and is found eligible, the CAO must take the following actions:

7 CFR § 273.14(d)(1)

Authorize benefits so that SNAP benefits are received no later than 30 calendar days after the date the household received its last benefit.

Send a Notice of Eligibility telling the household the benefit amount and the certification period length.

Example: A household is certified for one month only and receives April's benefit and a PA 10-SP on April 2. The renewal form was received timely, the interview was held, verification was provided on April 15, and the household was found eligible. The household must receive a notice and its SNAP benefits before May 2.

For all other households that complete the renewal process timely and are found eligible, the CAO must take the following actions:

7 CFR § 273.14(d)(2)

Authorize benefits so that the household receives SNAP benefits by the normal issuance cycle in the month after the last month of its current certification period.

NOTE: If this time frame cannot be met within the period allowed for submitting verification, the CAO must authorize SNAP benefits so that they are received within five days from the date the household provided the needed verification. The CAO must not prorate benefits.

Send a notice of eligibility telling the household the benefit amount and the new certification period.

Example: A household's certification period ends in November. The renewal form was received timely, the interview was held, verification was provided, and the household was found eligible. The household must receive a notice before November 30. The household is entitled to uninterrupted benefits for December.

At the end of the renewal interview, offer the client the opportunity to register to vote. If the client wishes to register, provide a registration form and help the client complete the form if help is needed. (See Supplemental Handbook Chapter 980 . )

42 U.S.C.A. § 1973gg-5(a)(2)(A)

OPS040503 PA 600AAB for the Food Stamp Program (Linked March 12, 2008)

Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing June 6, 2008

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act

body:
PFS-17721-506; Expedited SNAP benefits review for untimely SNAP renewals; October 2015

PFS12979576 Untimely Recertification Issues (Linked June 14, 2006)

PFS13048576 Renewal Form Received After Closing (Linked June 14, 2006)

OPS070214 Notice of Missed Interview (Linked March 12, 2008 )

OPS060403 Application Processing for Households and Families Recently Discontinued or Denied Benefits (Linked February 20, 2008)

PFS13000504 Application Processing Issues - OPS-06-04-03 (Linked February 20, 2008)

PFS13039504 Application After Closing/New Application Form (Linked February 20, 2008)

PFS13622576 Notice Of Missed Interview Language (Linked May 19, 2007)

PFS13664576 Notice of Missed Interview (NOMI) at Renewal (Linked May 15, 2007)

The household has not completed the renewal process timely if it:

7 CFR § 273.14(e)

Fails to submit a timely renewal form;

Submits a renewal form after the certification period ends;

Submits a renewal form timely but does not complete the interview scheduled by the CAO ; or

Submits a renewal form timely and completes the interview but fails or refuses to provide requested verification within the 30-day time frame.

If the household fails to submit a timely renewal form, the CAO must authorize SNAP benefits to an eligible household so that they are received no later than 30 calendar days after an untimely renewal form has been filed. (See Section 576.31 .) An untimely renewal interview will result in the proration of benefits similar to the initial application, except for migrant and seasonal farm workers. All other application procedures apply. (See Chapter 504 .)

7 CFR § 273.14(e)

Pub. L. 104-193 § 827

Example: A household's certification period ends September 30. The household fails to keep the scheduled renewal interview on September 11. The CAO sends a confirming notice on October 1 telling the household that benefits have been stopped. The household does not submit a renewal form until October 4. If the interview is completed by October 7, all verification is received, and the household is found eligible, then the CAO issues prorated benefits effective October 4.

When the household requests that benefits be reopened, asks for a new application, or submits needed verification within 30 days following the date benefits were closed, the CAO must reopen the case and re-determine SNAP eligibility without requiring a new application or renewal form if already submitted, ( see Section 579.3 ).

Once the certification period has ended and there has been a full month before the CAO receives a renewal form, the CAO must require a new application to be completed.

If the household submits a renewal form before an application form, the CAO must use the renewal form receipt date to calculate benefits. The CAO must attach the renewal form to the new application for auditing purposes.

Example: A household's certification period ends July 31. The household fails to keep the scheduled renewal interview on July 11. The CAO sends a Confirming Notice on August 1, telling the household that benefits have been stopped. The household submits a renewal form on September 6. If a new application form is completed, the interview is completed, all verification is received, and the household is found eligible, then the CAO issues prorated benefits from September 6.

If the household submits the renewal form timely but then fails to cooperate with the interview or verification requirements before the end of the certification period, the CAO must not provide uninterrupted benefits unless there has been an administrative error. If the household completes the interview or provides needed verification within 30 days of the end of the certification period, the CAO must reopen SNAP without requiring a new application ( see Section 579.3 ) .

Example: A household goes to its renewal interview on September 6 but fails to provide needed verification within the allowable time frame. The CAO sends a denial notice on October 5. The verification is provided on October 17. The CAO issues prorated benefits effective October 17 if a new application form is completed.

If the household provides the verification after the 30-day time frame but before the end of the certification period, the CAO must provide uninterrupted benefits.

7 CFR § 273.14(e)(2)

Example: A household's certification period ends in May and is in issuance cycle 7. On May 5, the household completes an application for renewal. The household is found eligible, but additional verification is needed. The time frame for providing the verification ends June 3. Verification is provided on May 24. The CAO authorizes uninterrupted benefits.

If an eligible household cannot participate within 30 calendar days after the application filing date because of the time allowed for submitting missing verification, the CAO must give the household an opportunity to participate within 5 work days after the household supplies the missing verification.

7 CFR § 273.14(b)

Example: A household’s certification period ends in April. The household keeps the scheduled application for renewal on April 1. The CAO worker starts to process the renewal with an issuance cycle 1 on April 20 and discovers that verification is needed. The CAO contacts the household on April 20 to request the verification by May 20. The household provides the verification on April 30. Because April 30 is the 30th day and benefits cannot be processed by that date, the CAO provides benefits by May 5. Benefits for May are not prorated.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act 1
heading: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act

body:
Although a household may lose its right to uninterrupted benefits, benefits must not be denied.

7 CFR § 273.14(b)

The CAO must issue benefits within 30 days from the application date once the household complies and is otherwise eligible. When the household reapplies within 30 days of benefits closure, the CAO must reopen the case without requiring a new application or renewal form if already submitted (see Section 579.3).
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act 2
heading: 576.41 Authorizing Untimely Applications, Interviews, and Verification

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 576.4 Untimely Renewals Due to the Household's Failure to Act

body:
A household that is ineligible for benefits because it failed to meet renewal requirements must receive a denial notice before the end of the current certification period.

7 CFR § 273.14(b)

The household must receive the denial notice no later than 30 days after the application or verification request if time frames for providing verification expire after the end of the certification period.

Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing May 3, 2007

body:
The CAO must restore benefits lost because of an administrative error if the household completes the renewal process before the end of the certification or within 30 days after the end of the certification period (see Section 579.3 ).

7 CFR § 273.14(e)

heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 577: Notices

body:
Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Handbook

Chapter 577: Notices
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 577: Notices 1
heading: 577 Title

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: Chapter 577: Notices

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.1 General Policy
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.1 General Policy

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.1 General Policy

body:
Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) On-Demand Language on the PA 253, PFS20792577 (Published February 23, 2022)

Adverse Action and Advance notice for SNAP recipients, PFS18931577 (Published July 11, 2018)

The CAO must give every applicant and recipient household a written notice whenever it takes any action regarding eligibility or benefit amount.

All notices must include, in language the household understands, the following:

The reason for the action

The Public Law, 7 CFR, or 55 Pa. Code citations supporting the action, shown as P.L. xxx-xxx , 7 CFR 27 x.xxx, or 55 Pa. Code 501. xx (The CAO must not use a SNAP Handbook section number as a citation.)

The household’s right to appeal any action taken by asking for a fair hearing

The telephone number of the SNAP office

The name, address, and telephone number of any person or organization that provides free legal services

The CAO must keep a copy of the paper or electronic notice for three years from the date it was sent.

Manual PA/FS 162 notices must include the mailing date, which is the first business day following the processing of the case record.

7 CFR § 273.10 (g)

PFS14486577 Stockpiled Manual Notices (Linked December 9, 2008)

Updated July 11, 2018 , replacing March 1, 2012
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.1 General Policy 1
heading: 577.1 General Policy

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility

body:
The CAO must send each household a completed Notice of Eligibility whenever:

7 CFR § 273.10(g)(1)

An application is approved;

An application is denied;

An application is pending CAO action (see Section 577.23 ); or

A new certification period is set.

The notice must be mailed no later than 30 days after the application date.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility 1
heading: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility

body:
When an application is approved, the notice of eligibility must include the following:

7 CFR § 273.10(g)(1)(i)

The benefit amount

The beginning and ending dates of the certification period

An explanation of benefits, if the first issuance covers more than one month

The name and telephone number of the person or office to contact for more information or to ask for a fair hearing

An explanation of the benefit that will be issued during the certification period, when changes are anticipated

The CAO may also include the following in the notice:

A reminder of the household's obligation to report changes

A reminder of the need to reapply at the end of the certification period

Any other useful information
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility 2
heading: 577.21 Eligibility Approved

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility

body: When a household is eligible for expedited service but must give verification to qualify for benefits beyond the first month, the notice must also contain a statement that the CAO will not issue the second month's benefits until verification is provided. If verification is not provided within 60 days after receipt of the application, benefits will end and the household must reapply.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility 3
heading: 577.211 Expedited Service

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility

body:
If a household is assigned a limited certification period, the CAO must include a Notice of Expiration ( PA 10-SFSP ) with the eligibility notice.

7 CFR § 273.14(b)
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility 4
heading: 577.212 Limited Issuance

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility

body:
When an application is denied, the notice must contain the following:

7 CFR § 273.10(g)(1)(ii)

An explanation of the reason for the denial, including the regulations on which the denial was based and the federal or state citation for those regulations

The name and telephone number of the person or office to contact for more information or to ask for a fair hearing
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility 5
heading: 577.22 Eligibility Denied

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility

body:
If a household is denied benefits because it failed to complete the application process, the CAO must send the denial notice on the 30th day. The 30-day count begins the day after the application is received. The denial notice must have the following information in addition to the information listed above:

A statement that the case will be authorized without a new application if the client provides the needed verification within 30 days of the denial notice mailing date

A statement that a new application will be required if the household does not provide the requested additional information by the end of the 30-day period

The CAO must give the household at least 10 days from the initial request date to provide verification. The 10 days may exceed the 30-day period.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility 6
heading: 577.221 Failure to Complete the Application Process

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility

body:
If a household is denied benefits because it refused to provide required information or verification, the denial notice must have the following information in addition to the information listed above:

A statement of what information or verification the household did not provide

A statement of what action the household did not take

The federal or state citation for the regulation that requires the information

If applicable, a statement of what the worker did to help the household get the information
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility 7
heading: 577.222 Refusal to provide required information or verification

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility

body:
When an application is held because of a CAO delay, the notice must contain an explanation of the pending status. It must be sent by the 30th day.

7 CFR § 273.10(g)(1)(iii)

When an application is held because of a CAO delay and some action is needed by the household, the notice must contain the following:

The needed action

A statement that failure to comply with the action within 60 days of the application filing date will result in denial of the application

See Chapter 504 to determine the difference between CAO delay requiring a pending notice and household delay requiring a denial notice.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility 8
heading: 577.23 Eligibility Pending

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility

body:
The CAO must send a notice when a new certification period is set. The CAO must send the eligibility or denial notice by the end of the certification period if the application for certification is filed by the 15th day of the last month of the certification period.

55 Pa. Code § 501.9(j)

The CAO must give a notice of eligibility or denial no later than 30 days after the last benefit to any household that received a PA 10-SFSP at renewal time and reapplied timely.

The CAO must not use the PA 10-SFSP to shorten a household’s current certification period.

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing October 25, 2008
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility 9
heading: 577.24 Certification

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.2 Notice of Eligibility

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.3 Advance Notice of Adverse Action
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.3 Advance Notice of Adverse Action

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.3 Advance Notice of Adverse Action

body:
Adverse Action and Advance notice for SNAP recipients, PFS-18931-577 (Published July 11, 2018)

The CAO must give every household a timely and adequate Advance Notice before taking any action to reduce or end benefits within a certification period . (See Section 577.4 for actions that do not require an Advance Notice.)

7 CFR § 273.13 (a)(1) and 55 Pa. Code § 501.9(h)

The CAO must mail the Advance Notice at least 15 days before the proposed effective date of the action. The day after the mailing date is the first day of the 15 -day time period. This 15 -day period gives the household time to exercise its right of appeal with benefits continued pending the hearing decision. If the CAO receives the hearing request on the next work day after a 15 -day period that ends on a weekend or holiday, benefits must continue pending the hearing decision.

To meet required time frames, the CAO may take action before the 15 -day period ends. If action has been taken and the household appeals within 15 days and has not waived continuation, the CAO must make sure that a supplemental benefit is available to the household by the normal due date of the regular benefit. The supplemental benefit must cover the difference in benefit amounts.

In addition to the information on all notices, the advance notice of adverse action must include the following:

7 CFR § 273.13 (a)(2)

A statement of the action to be taken

An explanation of the reason for the action, including the federal or state citation on which the action is based

The effective date of the action

A statement of the action needed to establish continued eligibility, if applicable

Information about the availability of continuing benefits if an action is appealed

The name and telephone number of the person or office to contact for more information or to ask for a fair hearing

The name, address, and telephone number of any person or organization that provides free legal services

The CAO must use the Advance Notice only to propose an adverse action after a household has failed to meet a program requirement. The CAO must not use the Advance Notice to schedule an appointment or to request verification.

Updated July 28, 2022, replacing March 1, 2012
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.3 Advance Notice of Adverse Action 1
heading: 577.3 Advance Notice of Adverse Action

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.3 Advance Notice of Adverse Action

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.4 Confirming Notice
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.4 Confirming Notice

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.4 Confirming Notice

body:
The CAO must send a Confirming Notice telling the household that an action has been taken when the benefit has been increased or when the benefit has been decreased as a result of any of the following:

7 CFR § 273.13 (b)

An oral request in the presence of the CAO or a written request signed by a household member or someone acting in the household's behalf

A report that a household member is institutionalized

A report of the death of all household members

Eligibility termination for a drug-rehabilitation-center resident or group-living-arrangement resident if the facility loses its state certification or FNS disqualifies it as a retailer

A report from a household member or someone acting in the household's behalf that a person has moved permanently to another county

Expiration of the certification period

Information provided on a SAR form

Changes in income or circumstances during the certification period that were anticipated, with notice provided at the time of certification

A SNAP mass change , such as an increase or decrease in the standard deduction or standard utility allowance , if the system does not issue the notice automatically

A cash assistance program mass grant change

A federal benefit mass change

An expedited longer certification period assigned to a household with verification postponed. (The household must have received written notice that benefits could continue after the application month providing the CAO received the postponed verification and could act on it.)

A household converted from cash or SNAP benefit repayment to benefit reduction because of failure to make repayment

Increased income due to receipt of a cash grant that was not counted in the benefit (This applies to joint cash assistance/SNAP applications where the notice told the household that SNAP benefits would be reduced if cash assistance was authorized.)

The client's whereabouts being unknown and departmental mail (such as CAO mail or client messages) has been returned by the post office or ACCESS cards have been returned by Central Office

7 CFR § 273.13 (c)

NOTE: The CAO must try to establish, when possible, the reason for the returned mail. A client could be hospitalized or temporarily incapacitated and not be able to pick up the mail. The CAO must exercise caution before closing a case because of returned mail.

The issuance date is the latest date by which a household must receive a Confirming Notice.

When SNAP benefits have not been accessed in the Electronic Benefit Transfer account for 180 days, benefits are moved off line and the case is automatically suspended. The system will generate a confirming notice to the household and a copy to the CAO.

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing October 25, 2008

heading: 577.4 Confirming Notice

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.5 Client Verification Request Form (PA 162-VR)
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.5 Client Verification Request Form (PA 162-VR)

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.5 Client Verification Request Form (PA 162-VR)

body:
The CAO must send the Client Verifications Requirements ( PA 162VR ) allowing the household 10 calendar days to resolve conflicting information when:

The CAO discovers that a household has not reported information that appears on the Income Eligibility Verification System (IEVS); or

The CAO discovers that information from IEVS conflicts with statements made by the household.

If the household refuses or fails to cooperate in resolving the conflict, the CAO must propose to stop benefits by sending an Advance Notice of Adverse Action.

7 CFR § 272.8(c)(1)

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing October 25, 2008

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.6 Notice of Expiration (PA 10-SFSP)
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.6 Notice of Expiration (PA 10-SFSP)

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.6 Notice of Expiration (PA 10-SFSP)

body:
Each household will receive a Notice of Expiration ( PA 10-SFSP ) in the automated renewal packet. The CAO must enter the date and time of the interview on the PA 10-SFSP during the automated renewal process.

A PA 10-SFSP is given at the time of renewal to a household that is certified for only one or two months. It may not be used to shorten a household’s current certification period .

The CAO must provide each household with a PA 10-SFSP during the month before the last month of the certification period. This notice must not be sent earlier than the month before the last month of the certification period.

When a cash renewal is due and the household also receives SNAP benefits, the CAO must not shorten the SNAP renewal period. The CAO must process the renewal for SNAP, suppress the notice, and reset the SNAP renewal date to the original renewal due date.

7 CFR § 273.14(b)

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing October 25, 2008
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.6 Notice of Expiration (PA 10-SFSP) 1
heading: 577.6 Notice of Expiration (PA 10-SFSP)

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.7 Notice of Denial of Expedited Service (PA/FS 163)
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.7 Notice of Denial of Expedited Service (PA/FS 163)

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577.7 Notice of Denial of Expedited Service (PA/FS 163)

body:
When an applicant household cannot be contacted in person or by telephone to be told of the expedited service denial, the CAO must mail the Notice of Denial of Expedited Service ( PA/FS 163 ) to the household. The PA/FS 163 notice must be mailed on the same day the CAO determines that the household is ineligible for expedited service.

The CAO must document that the household was informed of the denial. It is not necessary to maintain a copy of the PA/FS 163 in the case file.

The PA/FS 163 must not be substituted for the Notice of Eligibility ( PA/FS 162 ) that informs the household of ineligibility to receive SNAP benefits.

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing October 25, 2008

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 577 Appendix B: SNAP Client Messages

body:
CM 430 explains reporting requirements for households enrolled in semiannual reporting (SAR). SAR households are required to report when the gross monthly income exceeds 130% of the federal poverty income guidelines (FPIG) amount. The message contains a chart that displays the 130% FPIG amount for various household sizes. Each October, the United States Department of Agriculture mandates a change to the FPIG. CM 430 is mailed annually to SNAP-only and SNAP/Medical Assistance (MA) households enrolled in SAR. CM 430 is available in English and Spanish.

Click here for the English CM 430.

Click here for the Spanish CM 430.

Updated December 10, 2025; Replacing November 25, 2024

body:
PFS-22222-578 National Accuracy Clearinghouse (NAC) Alert Disposition Direction (Published July 22, 2025)

PFS-21946-578 Social Security Administration (SSA) Requesting Confirmation of Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Enrollment, Issued October 21, 2024

PFS-20723-578 The use of Social Media to verify Applicant/Recipient Information, Issued December 27, 2021

PFS-19818-578 Guidance on Verification Requirements during the COVID-19 emergency, Issued May 22, 2020

PFS-18914-578 Change to ARRC 123 alert due date Issued September 4, 2018

PFS-17638-578 SNAP Verification Requests and Rejections Revised July 30, 2015

Every household is required, as a condition of eligibility, to prove certain information about its circumstances.

Sources of verification include the following:

Written evidence

Collateral contacts

Home visits

Automated sources

See Chapter 578, Appendix A for a list of generally available verification sources.

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(4)

Updated September 5, 2018, replacing March 1, 2012

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.2 Household Responsibility
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.2 Household Responsibility

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.2 Household Responsibility

body:
The household has primary responsibility for providing proof (documentary evidence) to support statements on the application and to resolve any questionable information. The household's statements about their circumstances is not required verification in most instances.

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(5)(i)

A person can give written evidence to the CAO by mail, by fax, by e-mail, or through an authorized representative.

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(5)(ii)

The household may substitute a collateral contact for written evidence. The household is responsible for naming the collateral contact. The CAO must provide help if it is needed.

A household interviewed by phone is not exempt from verification requirements. The household must provide the required verification or name collateral contacts who can verify its statements. The CAO must not require a member of the household to come to the CAO to provide verification.

A household that refuses to provide required verification or fails to respond to a CAO request for verification without good cause is ineligible or does not receive the deduction, until the verification is provided.

7 CFR § 273.2(d)(1)

For applicants, benefits are denied or authorized without the deduction.

For recipients, benefits are terminated following timely notice or are adjusted without the deduction.

A household is required as a condition of eligibility to sign the Addendum to Application for Benefits ( AAB ) with the SNAP application and renewal forms.

NOTE: AAB language has been incorporated into the COMPASS rights and responsibilities section.

Administrative Notice 6-99, issued January 4, 1999

Reissued March 1, 2012 , replacing October 31, 2008

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.3 County Assistance Office Responsibility
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.3 County Assistance Office Responsibility

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.3 County Assistance Office Responsibility

body:
The CAO will provide an Appointment Notice and Verification Checklist ( PA 253 ), at application and renewal , telling the household what verification must be submitted to be certified/ recertified for SNAP benefits.

7 CFR § 273.2(c)(5)

The CAO will determine what information must be verified from:

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(5)

Information on the application

Existing case information

Statements by the household during the interview

Automated sources

NOTE: Beginning October 1, 2010, if a birth certificate is required for any reason for a Puerto Rico-born individual, CAOs must only accept birth certificates issued on or after July 1, 2010.

PFS 18433-578 ; Verification of Terminated Income, April 18, 2017

PFS 18434-578 ; No Income Reported at Application and Renewal, April 18, 2017

The CAO must do the following:

Verify changes reported on the SAR form at the six-month review, if required.

Verify inconsistent information.

Be flexible in accepting reasonable documentation from the household.

Not limit verification to a specific document or source but may accept more than one document or source for verification. This means that the CAO can ask for income verification but should not limit the acceptable type of verification to a single type of verification, such as paystubs .

• Example: The client indicates that they work at Giant Food Stores. The caseworker can request verification of income they receive from employment. The client provides a letter from their boss stating how much the client makes. This is acceptable verification.

• Example: The client states that they are receiving Social Security Disability benefits. The caseworker can request that the client provide verification of income they receive in Social Security Benefits if it can’t be verified through automated sources. The client provides a bank statement with deposits that indicate their Social Security benefits. This is acceptable verification.

• Example: The client states that they are self-employed. The caseworker can request that the client provide verification of self-employment income by providing a copy of their tax return. The client provides copies of invoices and receipts for the past month instead. This is acceptable verification.

Not require the household to present verification in person.

NOTE: An Authorization for Release of Information ( PA 4 ) is not a requirement for SNAP. A household cannot be denied benefits for failing to sign a blanket PA 4 . However, the CAO may use a PA 4 if a specific eligibility item needs a signed release form.

At the initial certification interview, the household will be given a minimum of 10 days from the request date to provide missing verification.

7 CFR § 273.2(h)(1)(i)(C)

At the renewal interview, the household will be given 30 days from the request date to provide missing verification.

55 Pa. Code § 501.9(i)

The CAO will help the household get proof of its statements, providing the household is cooperating, as specified in Section 504.15 .

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(5)

T he CAO will ensure the household, (who must sign the AAB), knows the impact of not reporting and/or verifying expenses.

The household must be informed of their right to report expenses to the CAO any time during the certification period .

The case record must clearly establish eligibility or ineligibility and benefit amount. The CAO will:

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(6)

resolve any conflicting information

explain in the case notes any decision to require additional verification or to verify inconsistent information.

explain actions taken in the case notes

Important: Once information which is not likely to change is verified and recorded in the case record, that information does not need to be verified again. Items such as the Social Security Number (SSN), birth date, and citizenship are not normally expected to change. However, the worker may require verification again if there has been a change or the original information was not correct. The decision to re-verify must be explained in the case record.

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(8(

body:
The CAO will follow cash assistance verification requirements for information that needs to be verified for both cash assistance and SNAP.

7 CFR § 273.2(j)(1)(iii)

If all SNAP verification requirements are met but cash requirements are not, the CAO may not delay issuing SNAP benefits beyond the 30-day application time frame.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.3 County Assistance Office Responsibility 2
heading: 578.31 Joint Public Assistance/SNAP Households

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.3 County Assistance Office Responsibility

body:
The CAO will verify the following information before authorizing benefits:

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)

Gross nonexempt income (see Chapter 550 )

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(i)

Exception: When an otherwise eligible applicant has cooperated but cannot verify income because a third party is late or not cooperating, the worker must use the best available information to authorize benefits.

Reminder: Workers should check PACSES for child support or alimony (even if listed as voluntary on the application).

Noncitizen immigration status of each household member identified as a noncitizen (see Chapter 522 and Supplemental Handbook, Chapter 740 )

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(ii))

Exception: A noncitizen is certified up to six months until a federal agency verifies citizenship status.

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(B)(3)

Countable months of SNAP benefits received by an able-bodied adult without dependents (ABAWD) from another state

Number of hours worked by an ABAWD

The obligation to pay utility expenses to determine the appropriate standard utility allowance. Verification is only needed if the information provided by the household is questionable . (see Chapter 560 )

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(iii)

7 CFR § 273.9(d)(6)

The obligation to pay utility expenses for a home unoccupied because of employment or training away from home, illness, or abandonment caused by a natural disaster or casualty loss, if the costs result in a deduction (see Chapter 560 )

Medical costs if they result in a deduction (see Chapter 560 )

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(iv)

Reimbursements for deductible medical expenses (see Chapter 560 )

SSNs reported by the household are submitted to the Social Security Administration (SSA) for verification by SSA procedures. (See Supplemental Handbook, Chapter 950 .)

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(v)

NOTE: The CAO will not delay certification for and issuance of benefits to an otherwise eligible household solely to verify the SSN of a household member. Most SSNs can be verified through the Client Information System (CIS).

Pennsylvania residence (see Chapter 523 )

Exception: The requirement to verify residence before authorization may be unreasonable in unusual cases. The CAO may postpone the requirement in situations such as homeless households, migrant or seasonal farm worker households, and households that have just moved into the state.

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(vi)

Identity of the person signing the application. If the application is signed by an AR, the CAO must verify the identity of both the AR and the payment name (see Chapter 520 ).

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(vii)

NOTE: Any document that reasonably establishes identity must be accepted. The CAO will not require a specific type of document, such as a birth certificate. A collateral contact is acceptable verification.

Disability as it relates to who lives in the household (see Chapter 510 )

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(viii)

All factors of eligibility for households that reapply following a period of ineligibility due to failure to cooperate with a quality control review (see Chapter 504 )

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(ix)

Legal obligation to pay child support to a non-household member, the amount of the obligation, and the monthly amount of child support actually paid to a non-household member

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(xii)

NOTE: If the child support is not verified but the household is otherwise eligible , the CAO will authorize benefits without including the child support deduction. The CAO must include in the case narrative the reason the deduction was not given. Failure to document in the case narrative may result in a quality control error.

All other information must be verified only if it is questionable and affects household eligibility and benefit level. The CAO will give the household the opportunity to resolve any discrepancies before determining eligibility and must explain in the case record why the information is questionable.

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(2)(i)

Information may be questionable in the following situations:

The application contains contradictory statements.

The client mentions another person during the interview and it sounds like the person might be living with the household.

There is reason to believe an "absent parent" is really living with the household.

There is a sudden absence of medical bills for a household member who has had continuous medical expenses for several years.

A boarder has the same last name as someone in the household.

Individuals living in the same dwelling unit but claim separate household status.

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(x)

The household reports income from a loan.

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(2)(i)

The CAO will verify address and shelter/utility costs if:

At application, the CAO must verify shelter costs if the applicant:

• Provided questionable information.

To be considered questionable:

• Information on the application must be inconsistent with other information on the application or previous applications.

• Information is inconsistent with information received by the CAO, known in the case record, presented during the interview, or provided from other sources.

Verification should be requested when information provided by the applicant is incomplete, unclear, or contradictory. When determining if information is questionable, the decision shall be based on each household’s individual circumstances.

Determination of questionable information shall not be based on race, religion, ethnic background, or national origin. CAOs should not target groups such as migrant farmworkers or American Indians for more intensive verification under this provision. The rationale for determining information questionable should be entered in the narrative.

Example: Ms. Smith applies for SNAP benefits. She is not known as she has never received benefits. Her shelter costs are reasonable based on her income reported. No verification should be requested since the information Ms. Smith provided is NOT questionable.

Example: Mr. Jones is renewing his SNAP benefits. He previously reported his rent as $350 per month. On this renewal, he reports living at the same address and reports a rent amount of $800 monthly. This information is considered questionable and verification should be requested. The CAO must narrate that information is questionable because client is reporting rent increase of $450 monthly with no change of address.

At SAR review or renewal and during the certification period , the CAO must verify shelter costs only if the information provided by the household is questionable.

Forms of acceptable verification for each allowable expense include, but are not limited to the following:

• Rent or mortgage

• Statement from the institution holding the mortgage

• Rent receipt

• Statement by the landlord or manager

• Current lease

• Property tax

• Bills or statements from the taxing authority

• Property insurance

• Bills or statements from the insurance company or a copy of a current receipt

• A copy of a current receipt.

NOTE: Insurance premiums on personal property and furnishings are not included.

NOTE: If the household fail s to provide the required verification for shelter expense, the CAO will compute the household eligibility and allotment without applying the shelter deduction.

The CAO will verify dependent care expenses if:

The applicant or recipient:

• Provides questionable information.

NOTE: The CAO will always review the Pennsylvania Enterprise to Link Information across Networks ( PELICAN ) to verify whether the household is receiving subsidized childcare and has a co-pay.

body:
Only identity must be verified prior to authorizing expedited benefits. The CAO will make a reasonable effort to verify all other items within the expedited time frames. (See Chapter 506 .)

7 CFR § 273.2(i)(4)(i)

body:
The CAO will verify:

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(8)

Changes in income

NOTE: Check PACSES for changes in child support or alimony

The obligation to pay utility expenses, if it has changed

Previously unreported medical expenses and recurring changes in medical expenses of more than $25

Changes in legally obligated child support paid to a non-household member

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(xii)

NOTE: If child support is not verified but the household is otherwise eligible, the CAO will authorize benefits without including the child support deduction. The reason the deduction was not given must be included in the case notes. Failure to document in the case notes may result in a quality control error.

If the information is incomplete, inaccurate, inconsistent, or outdated, the CAO will verify:

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(8)(i)(D)

Changes in residence

Obligation to pay utility expenses, if it has changed

Total medical expenses that are unchanged or have changed by $25 or less

Questionable shelter/utility or dependent care expenses

NOTE: The CAO will always review PELICAN for subsidized child care.

Other information, including unchanged information, is verified only if the information is incomplete, inaccurate, inconsistent, or outdated.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.3 County Assistance Office Responsibility 5
heading: 578.34 At Renewal

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.3 County Assistance Office Responsibility

body:
The CAO will verify changes using the same requirements as at renewal.

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(8)(ii)

Exception: For non-SAR households, medical expenses must be verified if the total expense has changed by more than $25.
citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.3 County Assistance Office Responsibility 6
heading: 578.35 During the Semiannual or Certification Period

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.3 County Assistance Office Responsibility

body:
The CAO will verify:

7 CFR § 273.12(a)(i)(vii)

Changes in the source of income

All gross monthly income

NOTE: Check PACSES for changes in child support or alimony

Changes in who lives in the household

Changes in residence and associated shelter/utility expenses, if they are questionable

Changes in vehicles

Changes in resources that reach or exceed $2,000, or $3,250, if applicable

Changes in the obligation to pay child support

Previously unreported dependent care expenses

Other information, including unchanged information, is only verified if the information is incomplete, inaccurate, inconsistent, or outdated.

Updated November 3, 2023, Replacing July 1, 2021

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.4 Documentary Evidence
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.4 Documentary Evidence

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.4 Documentary Evidence

body:
The CAO must not limit written verification to any specific type. Verification may be given by the household , the authorized representative, a person acting on behalf of the household, or a collateral contact .

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(4)(i)

When written evidence is unavailable, insufficient, or appears to be falsified or inconsistent, the CAO will:

Give the household the opportunity to provide verification; or

Obtain verification directly from the source.

Types of written verification include, but are not limited to, the following:

Wage stubs

Request for Employment/Earnings Information ( PA 78 )

Request for Financial Information ( PA 76 )

Rent receipts

Utility bills

Bank books or statements

Copies of benefit checks

Signed statements of third parties

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing October 31, 2008

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.5 Collateral Contacts
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.5 Collateral Contacts

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.5 Collateral Contacts

body:
A collateral contact is used when documentary evidence is insufficient or cannot be obtained. The collateral contact may be made in person or by telephone with anyone who can be expected to give reliable information, such as an employer, a neighbor, a landlord, or a community social service agency, etc.

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(4)(ii)

The household should name the collateral contact. The worker will assist if necessary.

If the household fails to name a contact or names one that is not acceptable, the worker will select one for the household.

Reissued March 1, 2012 , reviewed March 30, 2004

body:
The CAO worker may use home visits to obtain verification when written evidence or collateral contacts are not sufficient to make an eligibility decision.

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(4)(iii)

The CAO must schedule home visits in advance with the household and have the home visit reviewed/ approved by a supervisor if it is county practice.

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing March 30, 2004

citation_label: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.7 Pending Verification
heading: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.7 Pending Verification

metadata.title: Pennsylvania SNAP Handbook: 578.7 Pending Verification

body:
The CAO will not delay issuing benefits beyond 30 days for otherwise eligible households for the following verification :

Citizenship or noncitizen status. Certain noncitizens are certified for up to six months pending verification of:

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(ii)(B)(3)

Immigration status through the Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlement program; or

Veteran status through the Veteran’s Administration.

Social Security numbers. Benefits will not be denied for a household member if the enumeration requirements are met. Only the household member failing to meet requirements is disqualified. See Supplemental Handbook, Chapter 950 .

7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(v)

Reissued March 1, 2012, replacing October 27, 2008
